rationales.security: She is "running through the talking points" and would "rather have every answer ready than stumble," showing a strong preference for predictability and reducing risk.
rationales.conformity: Preparing so thoroughly for parent meetings reflects wanting to meet expectations and avoid the social disapproval of "stumble[ing] in front of someone asking about their child."

rationales.security: She felt "steady" during budget-cut fears because her "position is permanent," grounding her response in stability and protection from threat.
rationales.benevolence: After noticing newer teachers' anxiety, she "offered to help one of them update her CV after work," directly supporting someone close in her work community.

rationales.stimulation: She reacts to the move with "my stomach keeps tightening" and focuses on why they "do not speak German well enough," actively recoiling from novelty and uncertainty.
rationales.security: She wants "a guarantee" and emphasizes the "job, the savings, being close to my parents" that she has "built carefully here," showing strong attachment to safety and stability.

rationales.achievement: What bothers her is that Priya stepped in "in front of the resident" and "made me look like I didn't know what I was doing," showing concern with demonstrating competence and being seen as capable.
She "pushed for this placement" because she wants "to be in the ICU by the time I'm twenty-five," showing deliberate striving toward recognized professional success.
rationales.benevolence: She spent "twenty minutes" with Mrs. Okonkwo's wife, got her water, called her daughter, and says that caring side is "the reason I became a nurse in the first place."

rationales.hedonism: After "three twelves in a row," she still opened the study guide and skipped returning her mom's call, showing present comfort and enjoyment being deferred.
rationales.achievement: Even exhausted, she opened the "CCRN study guide," indicating continued effort toward professional advancement despite fatigue.
She immediately wrote down the attending's praise, "good clinical instinct," because "that's going in my ICU application," showing active pursuit of recognized excellence.
rationales.benevolence: She stayed "an extra ten minutes" with Mrs. Patel in overtime because "she needed someone to listen," prioritizing a patient's emotional needs.

rationales.achievement: She admits the "unit council looks good on my resume for the ICU transfer" and would take that yes back because it was "for me," showing career-advancement motives were active in her choices.
rationales.benevolence: Most of the yeses came from responding to others in need: she covered for Dana "because she was falling apart" and kept helping Jess because "if I don't teach her someone's going to get hurt."

rationales.security: She created practical safeguards by ordering groceries, signing Tyler up for meals, and setting a "recurring reminder" so she "won't miss another one," all aimed at preventing future problems.
rationales.benevolence: She responds to the missed appointment by arranging food and support for both Mom and Tyler, directly caring for close family members.

rationales.achievement: She studied for "three hours," set a "target" of 90%, and documented "meticulously" because ICU reviewers may inspect her charts, showing disciplined pursuit of excellence and advancement.
rationales.benevolence: After work she drove to Mom's and helped Tyler "outline a plan for talking to his teacher," investing time in a family member's problem.
Her stress centers on obligations to others: she worries about saying no to "a new grad who doesn't know how to read a rhythm strip" and says "saying no to my family isn't an option."

rationales.achievement: "CCRN practice test today, scored 88%. Getting closer" shows continued tracking of progress toward a demanding professional goal.

rationales.benevolence: She tells Tyler she'll "help him look at programs" and wants him to pursue meaningful work "without grinding himself down," showing protective care for a close family member.

rationales.power: He exerts authority by telling Sami "I need the incident report by Thursday" and frames the Aqaba issue in terms of control over the warehouse team.
rationales.benevolence: He put time into others' growth and reassurance, nominating Dina because she "earned it" and sitting with Layla after dinner to run through her recital piece twice.
He called Fadi in to recognize his work because he wants people under him to feel "actually noticed," and he is already thinking about how to celebrate Rania's "ninety-eight percent."

rationales.power: He explicitly sees influence in training Khalil: "The person who trains the new boss controls the narrative," and says making the first week smooth "positions me a certain way."
rationales.conformity: He decides, "I'll do it well because that's what professionals do," showing deliberate adherence to expected workplace norms and composure under scrutiny.

rationales.tradition: The entry centers on a multigenerational family lunch at "Teta's house" with "mansaf," grounding the day in familiar family customs and continuity.
rationales.benevolence: He spends the day taking the girls to their grandmother's house and being with family, reflecting care for close relatives.

rationales.power: His reaction is tied to ownership and control: "I've built this team from twelve people to forty-two" and "those are mine," showing strong investment in preserving influence over his domain.
rationales.security: He starts scanning LinkedIn because he wants to know what's out there "in case I need to move fast" and says, "I refuse to be caught off guard," reflecting a protective stance toward future stability.

rationales.power: He confronts Khalil directly, saying workflow changes need to "loop in operations first because my people are the ones executing these contracts," asserting authority over his team's domain.
rationales.benevolence: He is upset mainly because "I feel like I let Fadi down" and wishes he had "shielded him from this," showing protective concern for someone he brought into the company.
He recognizes that "I've been bringing it home and that's not fair to them," showing concern about how his mood is affecting his family.

rationales.hedonism: He turns the good news into immediate enjoyment by taking "the whole family to Abu Salem's on Friday night to celebrate."
rationales.power: The CEO says he "relies on my institutional knowledge" and wants him on the strategy committee, and he clearly values that restored standing and influence.
rationales.benevolence: Even during the celebration, he spends "thirty minutes on the phone" helping Fadi with a lease because "Family is family, even at nine p.m. on a Friday."

rationales.self_direction: She says "nobody asked what I thought" and stays silent while others change the schedule, showing her own choices being overridden rather than acted on.
rationales.power: The entry centers on being treated "like they forgot I'm his mother," which shows a clear loss of control over decisions affecting her child and time.
rationales.conformity: She "smiled" despite clenching her jaw, restraining herself to avoid open conflict and keep the household interaction smooth.

rationales.self_direction: She says "nobody checks if I want it first," making the core issue that others intervene without her consent or choice.
rationales.power: Her mother-in-law "walk[ed] in and lift[ed] him out of my arms," leaving her feeling dismissed in a situation she believes she should be directing.
rationales.security: She focuses on fever management, saying she knows "what dose of medicine to give" and when "we go to the clinic," which is active attention to safety and stability.
rationales.benevolence: She stays up "every two hours" with Sami and insists on being the one who knows how to care for him, showing close caregiving commitment.

rationales.self_direction: She protects a space for her own work by using "the only place I can close a door," actively carving out autonomy inside a crowded house.
rationales.achievement: She "finished a draft of the logo" and notes that "the base concept is solid," showing concrete progress toward competent performance.

rationales.hedonism: "Three nights of broken sleep" and having "nothing left to give" show a present life dominated by depletion rather than comfort or enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: She has to ask the client "for an extension on the logo revisions" and feels "pathetic" doing it, indicating a setback against meeting standards and deadlines.

rationales.self_direction: Even after being told the dress was not "appropriate," she says "I liked it, I bought it, I'll wear it," asserting her own preference.
rationales.conformity: She keeps doing Eid errands, tray arrangements, and family obligations despite frustration, showing active participation in expected social roles.
rationales.tradition: The day is structured around Eid customs and the rule that "First Eid is always with the husband's family," with her taking part in those established practices.

rationales.self_direction: Wearing the dress she chose leads to feeling "like myself for the first time in weeks," showing the importance of acting on her own preference.
rationales.hedonism: She laughs when Sami pulls a thread loose and describes feeling "steady" and like herself, signaling genuine present enjoyment and relief.

rationales.self_direction: She keeps the envelope because otherwise it "wouldn't feel like mine anymore," explicitly protecting a domain of independent choice.
rationales.achievement: She emphasizes "I earned it" and tracks the amount saved, treating the freelance payment as evidence of her own competence.
rationales.power: She wants control over the money and resists Tariq "help[ing] manage it," framing shared control as losing authority over something that is hers.
rationales.security: She saves the payment in an envelope and notes "I have about 200 JD saved now," showing active creation of a financial buffer.

rationales.tradition: She helps make mansaf and learns her mother-in-law's technique "with the wooden spoon," engaging directly with handed-down family practice.
rationales.benevolence: The day centers on patient care for Sami at the park and cooperative dinner help at home, investing time and attention in close family relationships.

rationales.self_direction: She quietly sketches courtyard patterns for "a design project," using the visit to feed her own creative direction on her own terms.
rationales.tradition: The entry dwells on the grandfather's story, the lemon tree, and tea made "the old way," showing connection to inherited family customs and continuity.

rationales.self_direction: She keeps pursuing paid design work because she wants "something that's mine," actively trying to maintain an independent creative identity.
rationales.hedonism: She commits to more work while "Sami's not sleeping well again" and says there is "no schedule that fits both," sacrificing rest and ease.
rationales.achievement: She immediately accepts a new client and frames the challenge as something she'll solve, showing drive to perform and produce despite constraints.
rationales.security: "80 JD is 80 JD" shows the decision is partly motivated by practical financial steadiness, not just artistic interest.

rationales.self_direction: She tells Tariq she needs "something that was just mine" and refuses to have him "fight that battle" for her, preferring to handle it in her own way.

rationales.conformity: She tells her mother-in-law "everything was fine" and Tariq backs her up, smoothing over tension rather than disrupting family harmony.

rationales.self_direction: He distinguishes between "what Opa would do" and "what I'd do," seriously weighing an approach that reflects his own artistic judgment.
rationales.tradition: The problem is framed against "what the shop is known for," showing active engagement with inherited workshop style and continuity.

rationales.self_direction: He creates a design where "the heritage is showing through underneath," intentionally shaping a hybrid approach that is recognizably his own.
rationales.achievement: He "finished the Ghent tile set," earns rare praise from Opa, and receives a solo line at rehearsal, all of which reflect strong competent performance.
rationales.tradition: He treasures singing music he's done "since I was fourteen" and values being part of something "going for hundreds of years," showing strong continuity with inherited forms.

rationales.hedonism: "Long day," a blown timeline, and not having it in him to answer Opa show strain and discomfort dominating the day.
rationales.achievement: He is still laboring through a difficult restoration where the plaster is worse than expected, indicating sustained effort toward demanding standards.
rationales.tradition: The work is on a church restoration, directly serving preservation of an older artistic and religious inheritance.

rationales.self_direction: He resists letting others define the work, questioning both productization and gallery framing in terms of what feels true to him.
rationales.power: He says what scares him is that once the work is out there, "I can't control what it means to people," making control over interpretation a central concern.
rationales.tradition: He is upset by mass-produced "Flemish style" coasters because "the hands behind it disappear," showing commitment to preserving the craft's lineage and integrity.

rationales.achievement: He spends the day at the bench and "filled six orders," showing productive, disciplined output.
rationales.tradition: The photo of his grandfather "working at the same bench in 1952" ties his present labor directly to family continuity and inherited craft.

rationales.self_direction: He calls the gallery himself, asks to see the space before committing, and keeps trying to define what "doing both" would look like on his own terms.
rationales.tradition: Opa's story about refusing a cheap department-store commission and the pull of "the family" and "the shop" keep inherited standards central to his decision-making.
Learning that his grandmother sang "that same motet" makes the music feel like "a thread" connecting generations, clearly grounding him in continuity with the past.
He worries the abstract work in that gallery would be "pretending to be detached from where it comes from," showing concern with staying rooted in inherited origins.

rationales.self_direction: Even while admiring older craftsmen, he says he would "suffocate" in that model, affirming the need for a path with more personal freedom.
rationales.hedonism: His shoulders are "wrecked" and restoration is consuming all daylight, showing the present is marked by strain rather than enjoyment.
rationales.tradition: He is restoring historic church murals and reflecting on craftsmen from "five hundred years ago," directly engaging with preservation and inherited communal art.

rationales.self_direction: He moves closer to saying yes to the Brussels show instead of taking the easy excuse to stay, indicating a more self-authored choice.
rationales.tradition: Opa frames the decision through "your grandmother would have liked that," and that ancestral blessing is what loosens his hesitation.

rationales.self_direction: He and others "pushed back" instead of quietly accepting the change, showing willingness to voice his own judgment against the proposed direction.
rationales.tradition: He defends the "old music" and questions why updating must mean "throwing out what came before," explicitly siding with continuity and preservation.

rationales.self_direction: He chooses a show of work that is "the conversation between" old and new, actively defining a path that bridges both worlds rather than submitting to one.
rationales.achievement: He finishes the south wall "just under the wire" and secures the Brussels show, both of which reflect successful completion of demanding goals.
rationales.tradition: He respects "faithful restoration," keeps the historic composition intact, and describes carrying the work "forward, not away," showing strong continuity with inherited practice.

rationales.hedonism: He balances duty with pleasure, noting that he and Nadia already booked "that new seafood place on the Palm" to "carve out space for the things that make life sweet."
rationales.conformity: He willingly rearranged the dining room twice and says there is "something calming about fitting into the role everyone expects," showing comfort with meeting social expectations.
rationales.tradition: He sits through formal family toasts and accepts "my uncle's stories about the old days" as part of honoring a major family anniversary.
rationales.benevolence: He spends the afternoon helping coordinate the celebration because he wants everyone happy and does not want to be "the source of that look" of disappointment in his mother’s eyes.

rationales.self_direction: He suppresses what he wanted to say and chooses the externally expected response, saying "that's not how things are done" instead of asserting his own view.
rationales.hedonism: After the bad meeting he comforts himself with pleasure, saying he ate "an entire box of those pistachio sweets" and is focused on taking Nadia to Bahrain for New Year's.
rationales.achievement: He lets Hamza present shared work "as if it was entirely his own" and declines to seek credit, even though recognition for competence is directly at stake.
rationales.conformity: He refuses to "create a scene" or embarrass a colleague publicly because it would look "petty" and "undignified," prioritizing social restraint.
rationales.tradition: He explicitly models himself on "how my father handled workplace conflicts," treating patience and restraint as the proper inherited way to act.
rationales.benevolence: He reframes the situation around team success, concluding that "the idea will get implemented either way, and that's what matters for the team."

rationales.conformity: He makes the "appropriate congratulatory noises," changes the subject, and avoids saying what he really feels because "you don't burden your mother" or disrupt family peace.
rationales.benevolence: He withholds painful thoughts both to spare his mother worry and because he does not want to give Nadia "another thing to worry about."

rationales.achievement: Once the difficult conversation is over, he is energized by returning to work and wants to make sure "the Oman route data is properly credited this time," showing renewed focus on competent performance and recognition.

rationales.benevolence: She spends the afternoon carefully helping Fatima through paperwork, going over "every section three times" so nothing gets flagged.
rationales.universalism: Helping an immigrant mother with a citizenship application feels like "something that mattered," reflecting concern for someone beyond her immediate household and her broader inclusion in society.

rationales.conformity: She stays silent because she is worried about "making things awkward," being "that parent," and embarrassing Mia, so she restrains herself to preserve social smoothness.
rationales.benevolence: She concludes that "the teacher needed backup and I let her down," recognizing that her choice not to speak withheld support from someone nearby who needed it.
rationales.universalism: Her anger centers on climate reality and its consequences, including Mia saying she does not want kids because of the future, showing concern for broader environmental harm.
She sorts neighbors' recyclables out of the trash because she cannot "let it go to the landfill" and keeps acting despite feeling her effort is tiny against a global problem.

rationales.benevolence: She spends the day caring for family needs and makes dinner for "a family from the resettlement center whose mom just had surgery," showing active support for others close at hand.
rationales.universalism: Her concern about fair trade coffee and ongoing support for a resettlement-center family both reflect attention to ethical treatment beyond her immediate family.

rationales.benevolence: She immediately says yes when Mia wants to help with the tutoring program, supporting direct care for children who need help.
rationales.universalism: The setting is "the refugee center," and she encourages participation in helping there, indicating concern for people outside her immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: She defends a life organized around what "feels right even if it looks like nothing on a resume," prioritizing her own convictions over conventional markers.
rationales.conformity: She rejects the standard that life should be measured by "a paycheck" or returning to "real work," pushing against the expected script for how her time should be valued.
rationales.benevolence: She defines her time around "showing up for families who have no one else," emphasizing direct support for nearby others.

rationales.security: He immediately calls the plumber, keeps checking the heater before bed, and fixates on the unplanned replacement cost, showing strong concern with household stability and avoiding disruption.
rationales.benevolence: His worry is tied to protecting family needs, especially because "Sofia needs new uniform shoes" and "Valentina has her graduation fees coming up."

rationales.security: He stays up at midnight running the numbers, weighing the emergency fund against extra tutoring, and insists that somebody has to stop problems "before they start."
rationales.benevolence: He is willing to take on more Saturday tutoring even though it cuts into time with his daughters because he is determined not to "put my family through that."

rationales.hedonism: He savors simple comfort and pleasure, from wanting to kiss the plumber in relief to enjoying an afternoon where "everything just fits into place."
rationales.security: The successful cheap repair brings major relief because the threatening household problem turns out to cost only "eight hundred pesos."
rationales.benevolence: The day centers on warm family care, including Leticia feeding Chema and a relaxed market trip with the girls.

rationales.security: His immediate reaction to the restructuring is to calculate risk, check savings, and worry that he "cannot afford any disruption right now."
He rejects Héctor's suggestion because he would rather keep union protections and avoid gambling on a new institution, saying he wants to "know what is coming."
rationales.benevolence: He hides the restructuring news from his mother so she will not worry and steadies himself by focusing on his daughters doing well.

rationales.security: The main emotional arc is relief that "my position is safe," and even his openness to change is deferred until "the summer when things are quieter," keeping stability first.

rationales.self_direction: He is already searching for programs abroad because he wants to author a life outside institutional routine rather than stay boxed in by the faculty role.
rationales.stimulation: The trip leaves him feeling "more awake than I have in months," and he contrasts that vitality with deadening routine back at school.
rationales.security: He explicitly resists the "stability of this position" and frames staying still as something that makes him feel less alive.

rationales.self_direction: He has effectively already chosen the consulting opportunity, saying that when he told Mei-Hua he'd think about it, "we both knew that meant yes."
rationales.stimulation: His mind is "already packing bags," showing clear attraction to a new, mobile project over staying put.
rationales.security: He is ready to leave the predictability of teaching for a modestly paid documentary role, favoring uncertainty over stability.

rationales.self_direction: He actively pursues unpaid leave for Sulawesi and admits he may not even want to come back, signaling a path organized around his own choices.
rationales.stimulation: The appeal is a two-month project in Sulawesi that pulls him toward new places and experiences rather than routine school life.
rationales.security: He builds a twelve-page formal proposal and keeps "building these safety nets anyway" to protect his position in case he wants to return.
rationales.conformity: He leans into institutional formality, carefully framing the trip as professional development to make it legible and acceptable to school authorities.

rationales.self_direction: He says staying was "never on the table" and that the real question was only whether he would leave "with permission or without it," emphasizing self-authored choice.
rationales.stimulation: He accepts immediately because two months in Indonesia means escape from the deadening sameness of "this building" and its repeated routines.
rationales.security: He describes leaving as something he would pursue even without approval, showing willingness to sacrifice stability for movement.
rationales.conformity: The response explicitly frames social permission as secondary, since he was prepared to go "with permission or without it."

rationales.self_direction: He insists "this IS my real life" and defines himself by moving toward what feels true to him rather than accepting his sister's definition.
rationales.stimulation: He says he feels most alive "when I'm moving, when I'm somewhere new, when I don't know what tomorrow looks like," which is a direct embrace of novelty and uncertainty.
rationales.security: He rejects "stability and proximity to family" and treats staying in one place as "slowly disappearing," showing active opposition to settled safety.
rationales.conformity: Hanging up on his sister and defending a life others see as irresponsible shows refusal to align with family expectations.
rationales.tradition: The conflict is specifically with the expectation that he should remain nearby as his mother ages, and he openly rejects that inherited family script.
rationales.benevolence: His sister's complaint is that their mother needs family nearby, and his response still centers his own need to keep leaving rather than caring for that close family obligation.

rationales.self_direction: He accepts being "one of those" perpetual travelers and continues to leave anyway, affirming a life pattern organized around his own impulses.
rationales.stimulation: The entire entry revolves around preparing for Indonesia and the repeated cycle in which departure makes him feel newly alert to the world before he moves on again.
rationales.security: He is again heading into a travel-intensive, medically precautionary trip and frames leaving as his default, favoring uncertainty over settled predictability.

rationales.self_direction: He says his whole life has been filtered through "will this let me keep moving?" and still chooses Indonesia even while questioning himself.
rationales.stimulation: He explicitly says "the unknown is the part I crave," showing that novelty and challenge remain central even during his doubts.
rationales.security: Standing still has "always felt like drowning," so the entry frames settled life as threatening rather than reassuring.
rationales.tradition: He recognizes that his mother is aging and wonders about another way to live, but he still continues the long pattern of leaving rather than honoring that family continuity.
rationales.benevolence: He admits he has spent most of adulthood elsewhere and cannot even name his neighbors, while his mother "won't be here forever," highlighting how close relationships have been deprioritized.

rationales.tradition: She works within inherited routines and references, from the parish hall's fixed setup to trying to paint the river "the way Grand-mere did in her later work," showing continuity with established family practice.

She preserves long-running family and community patterns, noting "the seventh year running we've kept to the same menu" and valuing the framer's use of Grand-mere's molding profile because "there's a continuity in those details."

rationales.self_direction: She rejects outside pressure from the Brussels gallerist and chooses based on her own priorities, saying "I told her I'd think about it, but I already know the answer."
rationales.tradition: She defends the parish hall show because neighbors remember "Grand-mere's work on those same walls," treating that continuity as more meaningful than greater reach or prestige.

rationales.security: She experiences changing the Sunday routine as threatening, imagining "empty chairs" and the table feeling "too big," which shows a strong need for stable, predictable family structure.
rationales.tradition: She argues that "three generations of Sunday lunches" and her grandfather building the long table are practices that "deserve to be honored even when they stop being convenient."
She explicitly ties her work to inherited craft, painting the same river view as her mother and grandmother and insisting "You inherit it by keeping your hands in the same medium."
She frames the test hang through continuity with family history, noting the light hit her painting exactly like "Grand-mere's piece in the catalogue photo from 1978" on the "same wall."

rationales.hedonism: She gives up immediate comfort and her own meal, saying "My own dinner went cold" after staying late to help a frightened mother, which shows self-denial rather than prioritizing pleasure.
rationales.benevolence: She stays past her shift to explain things in Arabic, fill out forms, and "just sit with her while her son slept," putting care for a vulnerable mother ahead of her own exhaustion and home life.
She centers her life around caring for family, saying "This is what I work for" and wanting her home to be "always open, always warm" for her children and loved ones.
She responds with intense caregiving to both families, holding Ahmed's mother while she screamed and later hugging Nadia tightly because she had "chosen to love other people's children as my own."
She interrupts her painting to feed Ma and reassure Xiaoli, ending the day satisfied because "Ma ate" and she "showed up where it counted."

rationales.security: Her first response to Yuwen's move is to ask about "salary," "housing," and "health insurance," focusing on practical safeguards and stability before anything else.

rationales.self_direction: She deliberately paints "with no plan, no composition sketch" and values how it felt like "stretching a muscle I had forgotten about," showing a return to autonomous, self-directed making.
rationales.benevolence: She repairs the call with Yuwen in a supportive way and also brings steamed buns to Xiaoli, investing attention and care in close family relationships.
She patiently teaches the children, guiding the youngest girl's hand through writing her name and valuing the studio's "fullness" because it gives her a reason to "show up" for others.
She hesitates to accept the gallery opportunity because she is focused on who will "sit with Ma the way I do" and make sure she eats, prioritizing close family care over professional exposure.
After Ma's fall, she stays all day, reads to her, cooks sweet potato soup, and concludes that "she is okay and that is the only thing that matters right now."
When Xiaoli calls crying, she immediately commits to shared care, saying "we will figure it out together" and planning to take more morning shifts with Ma to support her sister.

rationales.security: She creates a more stable care arrangement by hiring Ah-Fen "three mornings a week" and focusing on whether "this works out" so everyone can "breathe a little."
rationales.benevolence: She is attentive to Ma's dignity and comfort, pushing for the trial week and noticing Ah-Fen helped her "without making her feel small."
The visit is centered on close family connection, with Yuwen flying in to say goodbye and the two of them painting together "for the first time since she was a teenager."
She treasures the family gathered "at one table" and defines her life through caring for them, saying "I have spent my whole life making sure the people around me are okay."

rationales.self_direction: She begins a painting with no agenda, saying "Not sure why. It just came," which shows spontaneous, internally guided choice rather than obligation or external demand.
rationales.benevolence: Her reflections are anchored in mutual family care, from Yuwen stocking her fridge to everyone arranging support for Ma and the March show "without me asking."

rationales.self_direction: He celebrates building the business "on my own terms" with "nobody telling me which clients to take or how many hours to bill," showing a strong preference for autonomy.
rationales.achievement: Closing the deal "exactly how I wanted it" after years of "sleepless night[s]" reflects clear investment in excelling and winning on demanding terms.
rationales.power: He describes refusing to budge until "they came around because they knew I'd walk and they needed me more than I needed them," emphasizing leverage and control over the negotiation.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects pressure to "restructure the business" and insists he does not want "someone else's version of success stapled to my forehead," prioritizing his own path.
rationales.conformity: His contempt for family expectations and refusal to adopt the "scale properly" script show active resistance to meeting others' norms about career and adulthood.

rationales.self_direction: He ends a lucrative client relationship because the bureaucracy made him feel unable to breathe, and says "I started this company so I'd never have to sit in another meeting" like that again.
rationales.security: Walking away from "steady" money because the work felt stifling shows he is willing to sacrifice stability for autonomy.
rationales.conformity: Rejecting the client's committees, "alignment meetings," and calls to avoid "burning bridges" is a direct refusal to accommodate institutional expectations.

rationales.self_direction: He chooses the client that gives him "full creative control over the architecture" and says this is "the kind of work I got into business for," centering independent judgment.
rationales.achievement: Being treated "like an expert, not a vendor" and immediately engaging the "clean problem" shows a strong pull toward using and proving his competence.

rationales.self_direction: He chooses to spend the day figuring out the database architecture himself, describing a deep need to solve things independently rather than rely on others.
rationales.achievement: Saying that a solution feels "borrowed" unless he solves it himself shows he ties worth to personally earning mastery.

rationales.self_direction: He repeatedly emphasizes "I control the timeline" and that he worked late "because I wanted to, not because anyone was expecting it," showing deliberate self-authorship.
rationales.hedonism: The long walk, smiling at his life, ordering Thai food, and savoring a work rhythm he enjoys all point to actively valuing present enjoyment and comfort.
rationales.achievement: He notes "solid progress," finishing the schema, and securing sign-off on wireframes, reflecting concrete progress toward high-quality results.
He is determined to honor the timeline he quoted, even if it means "working through the holidays," showing strong commitment to performance and competence.
rationales.hedonism: Four-hour nights, holiday sacrifice, and pushing through exhaustion show active self-denial rather than protecting present wellbeing.
rationales.benevolence: He admits the crunch led to "snapping at the barista" and lying to Rachel about Christmas despite hearing her disappointment, showing his work priorities are harming close relationships.

rationales.achievement: He finishes the prototype under pressure and takes pride that it was "exactly what they envisioned," which reflects clear competence and delivery.
rationales.benevolence: He calls Rachel to apologize, wants to "make January different," and imagines sharing weight with "a partner," showing a stronger orientation toward repairing and sustaining close ties.

rationales.achievement: Staying late to run "another simulation batch" instead of leaving reflects prioritizing dissertation progress and work demands.
rationales.benevolence: He missed Layla's first swimming lesson and fears "the ones where they stop asking me to come," showing his current choices are failing the needs of those closest to him.

rationales.achievement: His day is organized around advisor demands, lab work, and TA prep, showing sustained focus on academic performance and professional progress.
rationales.benevolence: He recognizes that letting Dina handle the pediatrician and "everything outside the lab" has created an "imbalance," meaning his closest relationships are carrying the cost of his work focus.
Reading to Layla, holding Omar as he falls asleep, and taking the kids on Saturday so Dina can attend her class all show active care for his family and support for their needs.

rationales.benevolence: He sits with Mahmoud 'for an hour while he wept' and keeps worrying about whether they could have helped more.
rationales.universalism: His concern reaches to loss 'six thousand miles away,' and he is distressed by whether faster help might have changed the outcome.

rationales.self_direction: He says, 'I have spent so long making choices for everyone else that I forgot how to want something just for myself,' showing a loss of personal authorship.
rationales.stimulation: He does not move toward any new path and instead says, 'So I just keep not deciding,' choosing paralysis over change.
rationales.hedonism: The 'comfortable ending' with golf and retirement brochures feels like something imposed, and he cannot identify any version of the future he actively enjoys.
rationales.power: He feels 'asked to disappear into some comfortable ending I did not choose,' which frames the situation as loss of control over his own life.
rationales.conformity: He keeps deferring because of 'the people who want more of me,' letting others' expectations restrain his choices.
rationales.benevolence: His whole conflict is shaped by having 'spent so long making choices for everyone else' and worrying about what is fair to the people close to him.

rationales.stimulation: The offer to join an educational nonprofit in Morocco creates 'something like hunger,' signaling a strong pull toward a new and disruptive experience.
rationales.security: She names 'my position, my apartment, my carefully built routine' as the stable life she would have to leave, putting security directly at risk.
rationales.conformity: She questions whether she has been hiding behind 'responsibility' and family approval, pushing against the role others expect her to keep.
rationales.tradition: The dilemma is framed through family scripts like the engagement gathering and her mother's claim that Malaysia was 'irresponsible,' showing how inherited expectations shape the choice.

rationales.self_direction: She recognizes she may have been 'saving so I'd have an excuse not to take risks' and then asks herself, 'So now what?' That is a clear move toward owning the decision.
rationales.stimulation: Now that 'the net is built,' her attention shifts toward whether she will actually take a risk instead of staying in routine.

rationales.self_direction: She reaches out to Amir for 'more details' because she needs to test the choice for herself rather than just staying in the old pattern.
rationales.stimulation: She is pursuing the possibility of 'feeling alive' through the Morocco opportunity, even if only by opening the door further.
rationales.security: She asks for details because she wants 'a version of this where I don't have to choose between feeling alive and feeling safe,' actively trying to protect stability before acting.

rationales.self_direction: He stays because 'someone needed to be there' and dismisses the option of letting the junior handle it, subordinating autonomy to role obligation.
rationales.stimulation: He finds comfort in 'Seventeen years at this company' and even that 'The chair in my office has molded to my shape,' embracing sameness over novelty.
rationales.security: He works the holiday deployment window to 'make sure nothing breaks while the client's systems are live' and values knowing every hallway and system.
rationales.conformity: He fulfills the expected dependable role by showing up on a holiday weekend because that is what the job seems to require.

rationales.self_direction: He says he asked 'no questions' in the meeting and instead sat there 'nodding, taking notes,' suppressing his own line of action.
rationales.power: He worries about not making himself 'a target' and feels unable to ask whether his department is on the list, signaling a lack of influence over the situation.
rationales.security: He immediately checks savings and calculates how long the family would be 'fine,' making stability and protection the central concern.
rationales.conformity: His rule is 'you wait, you read the room, you figure it out,' which shows strong restraint to fit the social situation.

rationales.achievement: He 'Finished the API migration ahead of schedule,' which is a direct marker of competent performance.
rationales.conformity: He tells the girls it is 'three days, they'll survive' because they need to be 'presentable' for the visit, emphasizing expected behavior.
rationales.tradition: Preparing for Rima's mother and insisting the girls be presentable reflects deference to family norms around hosting an elder.
rationales.benevolence: He starts cleaning the guest room for the family visit, putting effort into caring for a close relative's comfort.

rationales.self_direction: When pushed to 'think bigger,' he just 'smiled and poured her more coffee,' withholding his own real response.
rationales.power: He feels reduced to keeping everyone else's life steady while someone else gets cast as the 'golden nephew,' which reflects low status and limited influence.
rationales.security: His private response centers on 'keeping her daughter's life stable,' making steadiness the value he defends.
rationales.conformity: He avoids open conflict with his mother-in-law by smiling, serving coffee, and letting the subject pass.
rationales.tradition: The whole exchange is structured by extended-family expectations and comparison at the family table, with him staying inside that script rather than breaking it.

rationales.self_direction: He looks at the startup role for 'maybe fifteen minutes, then closed the tab,' shutting down the option before really exploring it.
rationales.stimulation: The 'ground floor opportunity' is rejected because he keeps coming back to the possibility that 'there's nothing underneath,' showing active resistance to uncertainty and novelty.
rationales.security: He weighs the job mostly through risk, focusing on 'no guarantees' and the fear of jumping without a safe landing.

rationales.hedonism: Once the threat passes, he takes the girls out for ice cream and notices the bodily relief of finally 'unclenching.'
rationales.achievement: He 'Shipped the authentication module' and got a 'thumbs up from the client,' marking clear successful performance.
rationales.security: The central emotional event is that 'my department is safe' and 'No cuts in engineering,' producing palpable relief.
rationales.benevolence: He celebrates by taking 'the girls out for ice cream after work,' sharing the relief with his family.

rationales.self_direction: He 'kept my mouth shut and clapped when everyone else clapped,' holding back his own view in the public setting.
rationales.stimulation: He is deeply skeptical of the flashy rewrite and sees 'at least fifteen ways it would break our existing integrations,' resisting disruption rather than embracing it.
rationales.achievement: His manager relies on his expert judgment about the migration timeline and client risk, treating his competence as indispensable.
rationales.power: He experiences his role as 'the brakes on everyone else's engine,' which frames him as lacking the authority to set direction.
rationales.security: His main concern is what the company would 'lose' and the risk of breaking client systems, prioritizing stability and protection.
rationales.conformity: He avoids being 'the old guy who kills momentum' and publicly goes along with the room before speaking privately.

rationales.stimulation: Even in imagining what might be surprising, he says 'that's not me' about pushing for something risky, backing away from novelty and risk-taking.
rationales.achievement: The review says 'Exceeds expectations' and calls him 'the backbone of the engineering team,' directly affirming strong performance.
rationales.security: He takes pride in being 'a stabilizing presence during periods of change,' explicitly valuing steadiness.
rationales.conformity: What bothers him is that everyone sees reliability as obvious, which shows how fully he inhabits the expected dependable role.

rationales.hedonism: The entry lingers on the sensory ease of the coast road, Rima asleep beside him, and the simple conclusion: 'Good drive.'
rationales.tradition: Taking 'the girls to their grandmother's on Saturday' reflects ongoing family ritual and intergenerational connection.
rationales.benevolence: He spends the weekend transporting the girls for a family visit and sharing a quiet drive with Rima, centering care for close family.

rationales.power: He was treated as "background noise" during handoff and "did not say anything," showing a loss of influence in a moment where he expected authority.
rationales.conformity: He "waited until she finished and picked up where I left off like nothing happened," restraining himself to keep the interaction orderly instead of escalating.

rationales.power: He says the hospital is structured so that someone with his experience has "no more say than a fresh graduate," and the lack of control is "the part that sits in my chest."
rationales.benevolence: His frustration is tied to "patients who need updated dressings" and to getting "what my patients need," showing concern for people in his direct care.

rationales.achievement: He "caught a missing signature on one of the forms that would have bounced back from records," showing careful, competent performance.
rationales.benevolence: He took time to "walk her through the IV pump calibration again," directly helping a newer colleague even if he was a little impatient about it.

rationales.achievement: He defended the existing practice by pointing out it had kept infection rates "below the hospital average for three years running," grounding his stance in strong performance.
rationales.power: Despite his expertise, he was overruled by others with more institutional authority and says "we are switching," showing a clear loss of control.
rationales.conformity: He "gave in because fighting it further would have made me look like the old guard resisting change," choosing compliance to avoid social and professional backlash.

rationales.power: He interprets the request to teach the new protocol as "containment" after his judgment was overridden, which highlights reduced standing and influence.
rationales.conformity: He says "I will do it" and will "smile through it" even though he resents the situation, showing restraint in service of organizational harmony.
rationales.benevolence: He will "teach Hana and the others exactly how the new protocol works, and I will do it well," putting effort into supporting colleagues despite personal cost.

rationales.hedonism: After a hard shift he intentionally plans to "sit on the balcony with tea and not think about the hospital for a few hours," prioritizing immediate comfort and relief.
rationales.achievement: He "covered the floor mostly solo," kept patients stable, and finished the paperwork, which reflects effective execution under pressure.

rationales.power: He emphasizes "I chair this council" and says "I let him talk and then I steer the vote," showing a strategic focus on preserving control over the outcome.
rationales.conformity: He holds back from "shutting that down" because coming down hard would upset residents and threaten support, so he restrains direct confrontation.

rationales.power: He insists his request "should be prioritized accordingly" because of his years on the unit and wants his status to command deference rather than negotiation.
rationales.tradition: He appeals repeatedly to seniority and tenure, arguing that long service should still structure how benefits and respect are allocated.
rationales.benevolence: The day off matters because it is for "my daughter's university orientation," tying the conflict to showing up for a close family member.

rationales.hedonism: He enjoys a "simple morning" that includes stopping for knafeh and eating together in the car, leaning into a small pleasurable moment.
rationales.tradition: The visit features familiar family roles and customs, including his mother insisting "a mother's duty does not expire" and talk of a cousin's wedding.
rationales.benevolence: He takes his mother to her cardiology appointment before work and centers the morning on her wellbeing and companionship.

rationales.power: He sees the rotating role as a threat because "they want my title" and argues against flattening the hierarchy, showing clear concern with authority and standing.

rationales.self_direction: He "ended up just doing them because it was easier," giving up his own position instead of asserting what he wanted.
rationales.conformity: He chooses the path that avoids conflict with Sven, prioritizing quiet over pushing back.

rationales.self_direction: He wants to say he does not know if he wants to go back, but he stays silent because he can already picture "how her face would change."
rationales.conformity: He repeats "I would look at it this week" and withholds the truth to avoid upsetting his mother and disrupting the family dynamic.

rationales.achievement: He reports a "decent shift" with "twelve deliveries, no flats, tips were okay," showing satisfaction in doing the work reliably well.
rationales.security: He likes that the job is predictable: "You show up, you do the route" and "you just do the thing correctly and move on."
rationales.conformity: He says "Nobody asks you to have an opinion" and even when dispatch sends him across the city, "I just go," showing comfort with following expectations.

rationales.self_direction: He agrees with "yeah, maybe" and stays quiet about better rates even though he wants them, subordinating his own view.
rationales.security: He avoids signing because workers get "flagged as difficult" and says "Not worth it," prioritizing job safety over protest.
rationales.conformity: Rather than join collective pushback, he keeps carrying boxes and declines to challenge the platform's norms.

rationales.self_direction: He keeps his own irritation hidden because "bringing it up would be more hassle than it is worth," suppressing his preferences.
rationales.conformity: He apologises, turns the music down himself, and describes himself as the one who "makes the peace," showing repeated self-restraint for social smoothness.
rationales.benevolence: He takes responsibility for calming the situation and notices household needs before others do, acting in a quietly supportive way toward the people around him.

rationales.self_direction: He "sat there on the phone saying nothing" when asked about his plan, showing an inability to voice his own position.
rationales.conformity: His silence reads as deference to family pressure rather than resistance, especially after being confronted through Thijs and his mother.

rationales.stimulation: He goes out riding and even does three extra deliveries because "Moving helps," seeking motion and activity to break the emotional pressure.

rationales.self_direction: He holds back what he really thinks about living together because bringing it up would feel like "making it a bigger thing than it needed to be."
rationales.conformity: He lets Sven move the conversation along and avoids re-opening the issue in order to keep the meeting smooth.
rationales.benevolence: His unspoken concern is about the household dynamic and how to "keep everything stays smooth," showing attention to close-group wellbeing.

rationales.self_direction: She resists the market pressure to develop a "signature style," saying it feels "like a cage" and defending the freedom to choose different directions.
rationales.stimulation: She got "so bored" with the last series that she started experimenting with metalwork, showing a pull toward novelty over repetition.
rationales.achievement: She is actively submitting to group shows and wrestling with rejection in terms of artistic standards and fit, which reflects strong concern with succeeding in her field.

rationales.self_direction: She misses when she used to draw "just for myself" with "no thought about whether it would sell," affirming self-authored creative choices over external demands.
rationales.stimulation: She questions whether she needs "the burn to keep going," favoring intensity and creative charge over steadier repetition.
rationales.security: She explicitly doubts the appeal of sustainability, asking "What if I'm just not built for sustainability?" and leaning away from stability as a guiding principle.

rationales.stimulation: She says "I'm bored here" and wants the residency because she works better when "everything around me is unfamiliar," directly seeking novelty and disruption.
rationales.achievement: She spends the morning assembling application materials and even calculates a "fifteen percent success rate," showing a strong performance-oriented approach to her career.

rationales.self_direction: She says the client wanted "bold and colorful" and she "gave them exactly that," producing work she hates to meet someone else's brief rather than her own vision.
rationales.achievement: Even though she dislikes the result, she completed the commission and notes "Got paid though," indicating task completion tied to professional output.

rationales.self_direction: She spends ten hours immersed in a self-driven piece and values that "there's something on the canvas now that wasn't there this morning," centering her own creative process.
rationales.hedonism: She "forgot to eat lunch" and ends the day in physical discomfort, subordinating immediate comfort and pleasure to the work.
rationales.achievement: The entry frames the day around sustained effort and tangible progress on the canvas, showing a strong drive to produce and improve the piece.

rationales.self_direction: She wants to make "the best work I've ever made, whatever form that takes" and explicitly reframes style as "constraints I choose for myself," emphasizing self-chosen boundaries.
rationales.stimulation: She is excited by "the desert" and says she is "actually looking forward to that limitation," treating a radically different environment as creatively energizing.
rationales.achievement: She tracks her application "success rate" and connects the residency to making her "best work," showing clear ambition and investment in artistic excellence.

rationales.self_direction: He misses when he could "just have an idea, prototype it over a weekend, and ship it by Wednesday," showing a clear preference for autonomy over added control.
He pushes for independent action by saying "why don't I build a proof of concept this week," resisting a process he experiences as paralysis.
rationales.benevolence: He withholds his frustration because "she has her own stress with the kids' schedules and her job," showing concern for Maja's burden rather than unloading on her.

rationales.self_direction: He is "quietly looking at job postings" because he wants a place that will "actually let people build things," signaling a pull toward greater autonomy.
rationales.security: He weighs leaving against the apartment, school, in-laws' help, and a job that "pays well, has good benefits," prioritizing stability and predictability.
rationales.conformity: He "smiled and nodded" and said "stability being important" to match his father-in-law's expectations instead of voicing his real doubts.

rationales.self_direction: He concludes he may need to "work within the system instead of against it," which reflects suppressing his usual preference for acting freely.
rationales.conformity: He "did the thing" and produced the full documentation package, explicitly following the new process and meeting the team's expectations.
rationales.benevolence: When his daughter asked for help building a website, he "said yes immediately," prioritizing support for his child.

rationales.security: He describes work where "the algorithm deciding who eats" and earnings aren't even "petrol money," showing direct instability and threat to basic safety.
rationales.benevolence: He softens the truth for his family, telling Meera he made "decent" money and deciding Ananya "doesn't need the whole picture," to protect them from worry.

rationales.universalism: He helps at the distribution and stays focused on a worker asking for blankets "because the rains are starting," showing concern for vulnerable people beyond his immediate circle.

rationales.security: He immediately does the math on the school fees shortfall and weighs whether to take extra night deliveries despite dangerous roads, centering practical financial protection.

rationales.benevolence: He spends the evening helping Ananya with her Hindi homework, staying engaged with a close family member's needs in an ordinary but concrete way.

rationales.universalism: He is preoccupied with displaced workers and says the point is "not pretending the people in it are invisible," showing moral concern for strangers affected by injustice.

rationales.security: The offer matters because it is a "steady salary, PF, the works," and his response makes clear that "the school fees aren't going to pay themselves."
rationales.benevolence: He worries the job would mean he'd "basically never see Ananya on school days," showing concern about what the choice would cost his daughter.
rationales.universalism: He resists the job partly because "night shift means I'd sleep through Saturday mornings" when they prepare packets for the metro workers, showing commitment to helping people beyond his family.

rationales.self_direction: He keeps insisting "I need to think" and rejects the purely practical script others want him to follow, showing independent judgment under pressure.
rationales.universalism: He says he "can't accept that the answer to our problems is to stop caring about theirs" and is haunted by "watching people get erased," which is a clear expression of broader social concern.

rationales.security: He agrees to "do a trial week at Chakan" as a compromise after Meera argues for the steadier option, moving toward a more stable arrangement.
rationales.universalism: He tells Vinod-bhai he'd "still come for distributions when I could," showing he is trying to preserve his commitment to the aid work.

rationales.security: He experiences the paycheck and "PF deduction" as "a small wall being built between us and the next emergency," directly framing the job as protection against instability.
rationales.benevolence: He says rushing through Ananya's story and missing her dinners felt "wrong" and like "choosing a payslip over being present," showing the work is actively undermining care for his daughter.
rationales.universalism: He keeps seeing the camp photos and thinks "I should be there, but instead I'm forty kilometers away counting boxes," indicating his current choice conflicts with his concern for displaced families.

rationales.self_direction: He wanted to challenge the coordinator but "kept my mouth shut," suppressing his own viewpoint instead of acting on it.
rationales.power: He says graduating would feel worth it if he regained "a title that commands respect" and a role where others come to him for guidance.
rationales.conformity: "You do not embarrass yourself by complaining about circumstances nobody asked about" shows him restraining himself to meet social expectations.

rationales.power: He fixates on having once "ran teams" and had an assistant, while now depending on "a twenty-three-year-old named 'Brayden'" for basic help, highlighting a painful loss of status and control.
rationales.security: He is disturbed by abandoning "every standard you spent a career developing" and by a 10 PM meeting treated like normal, showing strong attachment to predictability and order.
rationales.conformity: He says he will "show up to the 10 PM meeting" and "be pleasant about all of it" despite resentment, prioritizing smooth relations over honest reaction.

rationales.benevolence: When asked why he told his daughter things were fine, he says "She has her own problems to deal with. My job is to handle mine," putting her wellbeing first.

rationales.hedonism: After a meeting that "went until midnight," he kept working and sent the outline at "2 AM," sacrificing rest and comfort.
rationales.achievement: He "drafted the entire project outline afterward because someone had to," taking on extra work to ensure a competent result.
He is energized by seeing that he "put something together that works" and that "the quality improved dramatically," showing pride in effective performance.
rationales.power: He reframes the moment as "build influence through competence rather than title," explicitly focusing on influence and standing even without formal credit.
rationales.conformity: He chose not to correct the group because speaking up would make him "the old man demanding acknowledgment," so he restrained himself to avoid social disapproval.

rationales.self_direction: He pushes back on the counselor's framing with "I am not pivoting, I am rebuilding," asserting his own interpretation rather than accepting theirs.

rationales.achievement: He received "the highest score in the section" and saved the praise calling his work "Exceptional," clearly valuing demonstrated competence.
rationales.security: He printed the comment as "Evidence" and "Proof" to look at on bad days, using it as reassurance and stability in an uncertain environment.

rationales.achievement: He wanted the group to rehearse and says he built his reputation on "preparation" and never entering a room unprepared, showing strong commitment to high performance standards.
rationales.conformity: He "wanted to say something afterward but held back" because they would see him as "the cranky old guy," restraining himself to avoid negative social judgment.

rationales.power: He says the MBA gave him "a credential that signals I am still in the game," treating status recognition as a key reason for returning to school.
rationales.security: He came back because he "could not stand being unemployed" and wanted "something to say when people asked," using the degree to restore stability and protect against vulnerability.

rationales.power: He says he would have done the consulting for free just to be "back in a room where people want my opinion," showing a strong pull toward influence, relevance, and standing.
He watches Matthias present "my framing" without credit and says he stayed silent, ending up feeling "small anyway," which reflects a loss of influence and recognition.
rationales.conformity: He stopped himself because speaking up would have looked "petty" or like "sour grapes," choosing restraint to avoid violating group norms.

rationales.achievement: He is actively pursuing the workshop proposal and worries about the March 10 deadline, showing goal-focused effort to move his work forward.
rationales.power: He admits he emailed partly because he needed Berger to "remember that I'm the one who builds things" and to think of him first, signaling a desire for influence and standing.
rationales.conformity: He deleted the follow-up email because he could hear how it would sound: "Eager. Pushy," showing self-monitoring to avoid negative impressions.

rationales.self_direction: He says "the comparative chapter is better work" but "I folded anyway," abandoning his own judgment in favor of others' control.
rationales.achievement: He knowingly chose the weaker path because "the politics mattered more than being right," which conflicts with pursuing the strongest work possible.
rationales.conformity: He agreed to "draft both options" because fighting would cost him more and he wanted to satisfy committee expectations and future gatekeepers.

rationales.power: Being introduced as "one of our PhD students working on something adjacent" made him feel like "a preview of not mattering," highlighting threatened status and diminished standing.

rationales.achievement: He decides that focusing on "the proposal deadline" is "a better use of my time" than chasing the funding decision, prioritizing progress on an important goal.
He takes on practical work from "reformatting citations" to already planning the reading group's logistics, showing organized follow-through on tasks.
rationales.power: He is eager to "handle logistics and speaker invitations," stepping into a role that gives him coordinating influence over people and events.

rationales.hedonism: She is still reviewing mock papers tonight after a day of meetings and says she only wants to sit with her chai for "five minutes," showing pleasure deferred to work demands.
rationales.achievement: A "long day of lesson planning" plus committee meetings and more papers to review reflects sustained effort toward competent performance at work.
She frames the threatened change against "twenty-two years of building this department's board results," emphasizing pride in long-term performance and standards.
rationales.security: Her distress centers on uncertainty and loss of predictability, and she says, "I need to find out what's actually being proposed before I react."

rationales.achievement: She highlights the percentile results and says, "This is what it looks like when the work actually pays off," treating the outcome as evidence of effective effort and method.
rationales.benevolence: She has been "drilling him on wave mechanics every evening for two months," investing steady personal effort to help someone close succeed.

rationales.achievement: The entry focuses on parents asking about "what rank" students can get and on being thanked for "board results," with recognition for strong performance clearly mattering to her.

rationales.stimulation: She considers the Bangalore role but rejects it because "the timing is impossible" and wonders if she uses the children "as an excuse not to take chances," explicitly shrinking from change.
rationales.security: She turns away from a higher-paying move because it would mean "uprooting Arjun" and disrupt Priya's board year, prioritizing stability over opportunity.
rationales.benevolence: Her decision is anchored in protecting Arjun's and Priya's schooling rather than pursuing the better-paid role for herself.

rationales.self_direction: She says it felt right to be "using my experience to shape the outcome" and defines the wrong move as "sitting quiet and hoping someone else would fix it."
rationales.achievement: She draws on "Twenty-two years" of experience and actively contributes a concrete curriculum proposal, showing competence-directed engagement rather than passivity.
rationales.security: She explicitly says, "What bothered me last time was the uncertainty," and feels able to work with the plan once it is concrete and predictable.
rationales.benevolence: She speaks up so "students aren't disadvantaged when they specialize," centering the welfare of those she teaches.

rationales.security: As soon as voluntary retirement comes up, she "ran the numbers" and catalogs lost medical benefits and housing allowance, showing immediate focus on downside protection.
rationales.benevolence: She recognizes that he "probably just wanted to talk" but she "turned it into a risk assessment," missing the emotional support her spouse was seeking.

rationales.achievement: She is fixated on the pilot going well, the annual evaluation, and the prior "exceeds expectations" rating, all tied to measurable success and recognition.
rationales.power: She notes the role brings "more visibility with the administration" and that the principal asked her to lead work that shows "the department runs on my curriculum design."
rationales.benevolence: She admits Priya is "quietly doing her work" and that she "should probably check in with her more" but does not because everything else fills the days.

rationales.hedonism: She chooses to "put the papers down" and let "marking wait until morning," savoring an hour that leaves the house feeling "the most relaxed" it has in weeks.
rationales.benevolence: She interrupts her own work to spend an hour on Priya's project and encourages her future by saying she could "absolutely" pursue research someday.

rationales.self_direction: Even while telling herself the timing will not work, she reads the entire posting and "bookmarked the school's website," preserving a personally chosen possibility instead of fully shutting it down.

rationales.achievement: She assembles evidence like pass rates, pilot progress, and olympiad qualifiers and says, "I know how to build a case," showing active concern with demonstrated competence and recognition.
rationales.security: She stresses that "the increment matters" because fees, tutoring, and coaching "all add up," linking performance directly to household stability.
rationales.benevolence: When asked what is actually the point, she answers, "Taking care of them," making family support the clear end goal of her work.

rationales.power: The hardest part is that Noor had "already looked up flat prices" and had effectively "decided without me," showing that being left out of the decision is what stings most.
rationales.security: Her London plan leaves him unable to sleep, and his reaction is driven by threat to the familiar family arrangement rather than excitement about change.

rationales.tradition: He dwells on maamoul, the kitchen smelling like when Samira was alive, and Hana shaping it "exactly the way her mother did," treating inherited practice as deeply meaningful.
rationales.benevolence: The whole entry centers on spending time with Hana and Tariq and feeling sustained by their wish to keep doing this together every Friday.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects the idea of letting Noor go and frames keeping people close as preferable to allowing the independent move his brother is defending.
rationales.security: His defense is about preserving closeness and keeping the family unit physically near, rather than tolerating uncertainty or separation.
He finally names the fear as, "if she leaves, the rest follow. House falls apart," making stability and cohesion the threatened center of the entry.

rationales.tradition: The significance lies in Noor cooking mansaf "from scratch" and setting the platter "the way I always do," a clear signal that family practice is being carried forward.

rationales.self_direction: He reflects on having once wanted "to study abroad" and worries he is now "doing the same thing to her," which shows growing support for Noor making her own path.
rationales.power: He criticizes the pattern of not letting children leave and recognizes his own impulse to repeat that control as something he regrets rather than defends.
rationales.tradition: He emphasizes that Noor "learned all of it here, in this kitchen, from watching me," focusing on what is transmitted across generations even if she moves away.

rationales.security: The looming scholarship is painful because "the house will be so quiet without her," and saying yes feels too final, showing strong attachment to a stable home life.
rationales.tradition: The entry lingers on Eid preparations, shared food, and a full multigenerational house that felt "exactly right," grounding meaning in established family ritual.
rationales.benevolence: His ideal moment is everyone gathered together and Noor helping in the kitchen, reflecting deep fulfillment in caring for and being surrounded by close family.

rationales.self_direction: He tells Noor she "should accept the scholarship" and even says she should return only "when she wants to," explicitly backing her independent choice.
rationales.power: By telling her not to come back out of obligation, he gives up the claim to direct her movements for his own comfort.
rationales.benevolence: Despite the pain, he supports what is best for Noor and sits with her grief tenderly at the kitchen table rather than pulling her back.

rationales.self_direction: He actively helps compare rental listings and reflects that "you hold something tightly and then you open your hand," showing growing acceptance of Noor's independent move.
rationales.tradition: Checking which neighborhoods have "halal shops nearby" shows he wants her new life to stay connected to familiar cultural practice.
rationales.benevolence: He spends time helping Noor plan the move and agrees to the family group chat so the relationship can stay close across distance.

rationales.hedonism: She celebrates finishing the series with "a glass of wine" and a night at "that rooftop bar," saying the enjoyment "wasn't a detour from the work. It was the whole point of the work."
rationales.achievement: Completing "six canvases" after "four months of work" and preparing for a "gallery pitch tomorrow" shows clear investment in producing and advancing recognized work.

rationales.hedonism: She leans into immediate comfort by "doom-scrolling" and ordering "delivery twice" instead of pushing herself toward work.
rationales.achievement: She explicitly avoids the commission she has "been putting off" and admits "wallowing isn't productive," which shows active withdrawal from competence and progress.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the in-house design roles because office life made everything feel "grey" and says "I'd rather figure out rent every month than wake up numb," prioritizing an autonomous life over a conventional one.
rationales.hedonism: The day centers on pleasurable, absorbing experience: "made coffee the slow way," sketched freely, and says "I feel like myself" in that unstructured moment.
rationales.security: She knowingly refuses "steady income" and "health insurance," even saying she'd "rather figure out rent every month," which actively sacrifices stability.
rationales.conformity: She resists the expected professional path her friend keeps recommending and refuses to return to the office life "people do all the time," despite social pressure.
rationales.tradition: She frames quitting her uncle's firm against family advice as worthwhile and continues to reject the more established family-approved path.

rationales.hedonism: She "celebrated" the approval by buying the art set she'd "been eyeing for months," choosing an enjoyable reward in the moment.
rationales.achievement: "Commission client approved the sketches" is a direct sign that her work met an external standard and was recognized.

rationales.self_direction: She insists "I'm not delaying life — I'm living it" and refuses to "build a life around a future version of myself," affirming her own chosen path over outside expectations.
rationales.hedonism: She values "watching sunsets from my balcony" and spending "three hours getting a shadow exactly right" over the family life she is being pushed toward.
rationales.conformity: She pushes back on the implied expectation to have children and says she prefers what she finds beautiful over "what I'm supposed to find meaningful."
rationales.tradition: The tension is explicitly with her mother and a more conventional family timeline; she declines that inherited script rather than honoring it.

rationales.stimulation: She tries a new medium at a "ceramics workshop" despite reluctance, then embraces the novelty of being "covered in clay" and experimenting with texture.
rationales.hedonism: The experience is framed around immediate enjoyment: "laughing at my lopsided bowl" and calling making things with a friend "one of the best feelings in the world."
rationales.benevolence: The day is meaningful partly because she is "talking to a friend," and the shared creative time with Nadia is treated as something deeply nourishing.

rationales.self_direction: She refuses the family firm's offer because "three years of building something of my own doesn't disappear," clearly defending her independent path.
rationales.conformity: Ignoring an offer sent by her uncle and cc'd to her father rejects a strong social expectation to take the respectable family-approved job.
rationales.tradition: The pressure comes through family channels, and she resists being folded back into the family business instead of following that established route.

rationales.self_direction: Even while scared, she reaffirms the artistic life she chose, saying the version with savings would also lack "the coastal series" and "the feeling I get when a piece finally works."
rationales.hedonism: She continues to privilege the felt rewards of art, especially "the feeling I get when a piece finally works," over the more practical life she could have had.
rationales.security: The entry is dominated by instability: "2am math," "minimum payments," and uncertainty about making it to "the 15th without another gig."

rationales.hedonism: She responds with pure delight, saying she "screamed" and then booked dinner at "the expensive one" because the moment was "worth celebrating."
rationales.achievement: A gallery reaching out because they saw her work on Instagram is explicit external recognition that the work was "worth showing."
rationales.conformity: She knowingly ignores the prudent expectation that "My mother would say I should save the money" and spends freely to celebrate instead.
rationales.benevolence: Her first impulse is to call Nadia so they can "scream together," making the milestone relational rather than purely solitary.

rationales.self_direction: When Principal Rojas pushes her about the department head role, she answers that she's "focused on my students," asserting her own priorities instead of following the expected advancement path.
rationales.achievement: She declines to pursue the higher-status department head position, which shows active movement away from career advancement and external recognition.
rationales.benevolence: Her stated reason is direct care for "my students," prioritizing their needs over her own promotion.

rationales.conformity: Even when Sofia's mother presses her, she restrains herself, saying "I wanted to say" more but instead "just nodded and promised to look at the essay draft again."
rationales.benevolence: She spends "two hours explaining the rubric" and offering individual help, then keeps thinking about how "Diego needs me more," showing concrete care for students.
rationales.universalism: Her concern that "the system is set up for families who already have resources" reflects attention to structural inequality beyond any one student.

rationales.achievement: Although she notes the role "pays more" and would "look better on my resume," the entry centers on why she may not take it, pulling away from personal advancement.
rationales.power: She treats the chance for "more influence" as something she may forgo because she is reluctant to step into that broader authority role.
