rationales.power: She says the hardest part is "not deciding" and that if she could "just approve it myself, it would have been done days ago." The entry strongly emphasizes wanting control over an important decision.
rationales.security: She submitted the request for "two additional night staff over the holiday period" because otherwise she will have to "cover the gaps." The motivation is clearly to secure safer, more stable ward coverage.

rationales.conformity: She says she "gave in because fighting about it at Christmas dinner felt worse than conceding." That is a deliberate choice to restrain herself to preserve social harmony.
rationales.benevolence: Even though she did not believe the concession, she stopped arguing with her son in order to avoid escalating the family conflict at Christmas. The decision prioritizes the relationship over winning the point.

rationales.power: She felt good about being "consulted, properly consulted" by someone who "respects what I bring to the conversation." The entry shows clear importance placed on having her judgment carry weight.
rationales.tradition: She warmly notes that her son made "his father's old Kartoffelsalat recipe" on Christmas morning. The entry values continuity with family practice and the past.
rationales.benevolence: She spent an hour with her son comparing insurance policies after realizing he genuinely wanted her help. That is attentive support for a close family member.

rationales.conformity: He "didn't say a word" because "nobody wants that fight at the table," explicitly prioritizing social smoothness over speaking up.
rationales.universalism: He stayed silent while someone attacked a "refugee housing project," leaving concern for vulnerable people undefended in the moment.

rationales.security: He chose the cheaper supplier because it was "enough to actually turn a real profit" and justified it as necessary so the business could "keep the lights on."
rationales.universalism: He placed the order despite reports that the factory was "dumping chemical waste into a river that feeds into local farmland," accepting likely environmental harm for business survival.

rationales.security: He rejects the accountant's plan that would put the company "in the black" and admits he will "scramble and stress" instead of choosing the safer financial path.
rationales.universalism: He refuses to pause donations after reading that a girl's rashes stopped once her family got a filter, keeping support flowing to people beyond his immediate circle.
He stops to check on installed units at Warm Springs and is renewed by seeing "the water is clean" and a family using it to grow tomatoes, showing commitment to broader community welfare.
He spends the day coordinating donation units and resists turning "people's water problems" into marketing, pushing for an opt-in approach that respects affected communities.

rationales.security: He switches to a domestic supplier that "costs more" and accepts that "margins will be tight for a while," choosing a less stable financial option.
rationales.universalism: He canceled the Guangzhou order after being unable to live with the environmental concerns, choosing the option he could "actually sleep" with.

rationales.achievement: The "exhibition proposal went through" after "three years of work," marking clear success for a long-term creative effort.
rationales.security: She is relieved that the grant means she can "stop checking my account every morning," showing the value of financial stability and predictability.
rationales.universalism: The installation centers on "rising waters" and "families in Bangladesh," focusing her work on climate harm and people beyond her immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the idea of giving up art for "a real job" and says she keeps "doing both," insisting on her own path despite pressure.
rationales.security: She dwells on being "too old for this uncertainty" and still chooses a precarious artistic life over a more stable one.
rationales.universalism: Her work is driven by "millions displaced, coastlines eroding," and she wants to make "something true" while "the world burns."
She is shaken by "another boat capsized in the Mediterranean" and frames her art around refugees' names, stories, and whether the work is useful to people like Fatima.

rationales.benevolence: She buys presents for her mother and chooses "small things" that fit what "she doesn't want much anymore," showing attentive care for a close family member.

rationales.universalism: She says she keeps "paying attention to what hurts" and explains that research about displacement makes her feel the world's pain as her own, showing deep identification with broader human suffering.

rationales.self_direction: Even after funding pressure to make the work lighter, she decides "the work will be what it is" and would rather risk disapproval than sand down the truth.

rationales.security: She explicitly values "Two more years of this stability" and being able to focus on the work instead of worrying about money.
rationales.universalism: She adds "a wall of handwritten messages from people who've rebuilt their lives after displacement," centering resilience and dignity for affected strangers.

rationales.hedonism: He plans "four extra hours a day" despite his back already acting up, sacrificing comfort and rest rather than protecting his own wellbeing.
rationales.benevolence: He is willing to take on those extra late-night slots so he can cover Priya's increased college fees instead of telling Amma to find another way.

rationales.achievement: He treats the renovation offer as "the first real opening in months" and clearly values being recognized as capable of more skilled work.
rationales.security: The appeal of the job is that it is "steady weekend work" with better pay, which would let him cover Priya's fees without "grinding myself into the ground."

rationales.hedonism: After hitting his target early, he uses the extra time for curry puffs and chess, saying Uncle Rajan's laughter is "worth more than winning."
rationales.benevolence: He spends his free time bringing snacks to "the uncles who play chess downstairs," investing in warm, low-stakes care for familiar people around him.
He stayed on the phone with Priya for "forty minutes just letting her talk" after her distressing night, prioritizing support for his sister even with an early shift ahead.

rationales.achievement: Mr. Tan tells him "you clearly know what you're doing," and the entry centers on the pride of being seen as competent by a boss figure.
rationales.security: He immediately calculates that the higher hourly rate means he could "actually rest on Sundays for once," framing the new arrangement as more sustainable and stable.

rationales.hedonism: He works "late night slots three days in a row" until his "knee is swollen," clearly neglecting comfort and immediate wellbeing.
rationales.security: He books "the polyclinic appointment for Monday" and accepts help for his back, taking a concrete step toward protecting his health.
rationales.benevolence: He covers Suresh's shifts because "Suresh helped me when I first started," and he keeps Priya's money to avoid rejecting her effort and hurting her feelings.

rationales.self_direction: She suppresses what "my hand knows what it wants to make" because "the brief says otherwise," yielding her own vision to external control.
rationales.conformity: She says "you read the room, you adjust, you keep the project alive," showing deliberate restraint to fit expectations and avoid conflict.

rationales.self_direction: She envies David's freedom and says she learned "all the reasons to hold back," which points to her own retreat from saying what she needs to say.
rationales.universalism: She is moved by a collage about "neighborhood flooding" made without sanitizing the damage, showing concern for environmental harm and its impact on a community.

rationales.self_direction: She embraces a project with "no committee, no brief, no polite rewrites" and says yes immediately, choosing freer expression over controlled approval.
rationales.universalism: She joins a march about "deforestation in Guaviare," studies satellite images of the cleared forest, and centers a family who may have lost their farm.

rationales.self_direction: She "compromised" by softening the contamination imagery and later admits her work has become "quieter" with "edges filed down," showing a loss of direct self-expression.
rationales.conformity: She frames public art as "negotiation" where you "serve the community's vision, not just your own," and accepts a safer version to meet others' expectations.

rationales.self_direction: She deletes three drafted replies and ends with a "sad emoji," calling the silence "utterly hollow" instead of saying what she believes.
rationales.conformity: She stays quiet because she can already picture "the awkward silence at Easter dinner," restraining herself to preserve family harmony.
rationales.universalism: Her unsent response centers "logging companies," "lack of enforcement," and families "being displaced," showing concern for broader injustice beyond herself.

rationales.self_direction: She says the march mural is "honest work" and that "the paint is saying exactly what I mean," indicating direct, unfiltered expression of her own view.

rationales.conformity: She reassures her mother by saying it is "just a painting, nothing political," softening the truth to avoid upsetting her and keep things smooth.
rationales.benevolence: She says "she doesn't need to carry that" and hides part of the truth because "she has enough," prioritizing her mother's emotional burden.

rationales.hedonism: She takes simple pleasure in the physical routine of "mixed pigments and cleaned brushes" and says "Sometimes that's enough," emphasizing immediate comfort and relief.

rationales.self_direction: Even while afraid, she keeps returning to the idea that "this matters more than anyone's comfort, including mine" and wrestles with showing up visibly for what she believes.
rationales.security: Being visible feels like "standing in traffic," and she fixates on her name being recorded and shared, yet she is moving toward exposure rather than safety.
rationales.universalism: Her mural depicts "the forest canopy dissolving into ash" and families displaced at the border of "green and grey," tying her public action to environmental loss and human harm.

rationales.self_direction: She follows through and speaks at the march despite fear, then notes "I'm glad I did it once," showing she acted on her convictions rather than staying hidden.
rationales.security: She speaks after imagining family backlash and says she had built "this whole catastrophe" in her head, accepting vulnerability instead of retreating to safety.
rationales.universalism: Her remarks focus on "someone losing their home" and on art giving form to harms caused by distant decisions, showing concern for people and places beyond her immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: She revised the protocol on her own time because "nobody asked me to," taking independent initiative beyond her assigned role.
rationales.achievement: She spent three months researching and refining the protocol to "prove that I could contribute beyond bedside care," and senior praise makes it feel like something she made mattered.
She takes extra shifts because "being reliable matters when you're trying to move up," explicitly tying overwork to advancement.
rationales.benevolence: During the new grad's first code, she "had to talk her through it step by step," providing direct support in a high-stakes moment.

rationales.achievement: She keeps checking for the "junior ward manager posting" and refreshes the intranet repeatedly, showing preoccupation with promotion and measurable career progress.

rationales.hedonism: She keeps checking work emails at home at 9pm, letting work intrude on rest and home life rather than protecting present comfort.
rationales.achievement: She says falling behind means "being invisible" and that she "can't just be average," revealing a strong drive to stand out through performance.

rationales.hedonism: She works from "6am" until "almost midnight," skips full breaks, and cannot remember her last actual meal during a shift, clearly sacrificing comfort and recovery.
rationales.achievement: She keeps pushing toward the review because she wants "the title" and "the recognition" as proof that all the effort mattered.
She accepts the training because it is "exposure" and "a chance to show the department head what I can do," using it to advance her standing.
rationales.conformity: Although annoyed, she says "I said yes. What else am I going to say?" and treats compliance as the expected route to getting ahead.

rationales.achievement: She hesitates to commit to the wedding because she is still orienting around her shifts and work obligations, even though she "could probably make it work."
rationales.benevolence: She knows she can likely attend but still avoids committing to Thomas's family event, leaving him to go to bed without reassurance.

rationales.achievement: She treats praise from "all four new hires" as a "Small win," showing satisfaction in performing competently and being recognized for it.
rationales.benevolence: A new hire says she made the protocols "actually make sense," indicating she invested effort in helping others learn and succeed.
She forgot their anniversary after dismissing the reminder during a shift, then realizes "I should have remembered," showing she neglected an important close relationship.

rationales.achievement: She spends the evening polishing a self-assessment that lists "all the overtime, the certifications, the protocol work," explicitly organizing evidence of competence for her review.

rationales.self_direction: She immerses herself in her own creative work, saying "This is why I do this" as she chases a glaze effect for months. The entry centers on making art for its own sake, on her own terms.
rationales.hedonism: She lingers over sensory pleasure in the studio, describing the light on the glaze and how she "forgot about everything else." The stop for knafeh also reinforces present-focused enjoyment.
rationales.benevolence: She immediately interrupts her work when "Mama called" and drives her across town to the pharmacy. The choice to help her mother without hesitation shows active care for a close family member.

rationales.self_direction: She says yes to the painting request because "what else do you say" with family watching, even though she is overloaded and resentful. Her own needs are deferred rather than self-authored.
rationales.hedonism: The "glass of wine on the balcony" is described as "the best part of my whole week," and she longs for a Dead Sea trip to "float in the water, eat too much, laugh at nothing." She explicitly frames pleasure and relief as necessary, not trivial.
rationales.conformity: She agrees to take on unpaid work largely because family pressure makes refusal socially difficult. The choice is shaped by not wanting to upset the room rather than by preference.
rationales.tradition: The obligation is framed as what family asks "because we share blood," and she treats Nadia's hardship as a duty she should answer. That reflects deference to familial expectations and kinship obligations.
rationales.benevolence: Despite exhaustion, she still takes on the painting because Nadia is "going through a divorce" and "everyone's been worried about her." She is actively trying to support a struggling relative.
She invests care in the workshop and notices Sana's pride in detail, highlighting satisfaction in nurturing children. The entry centers on helping others close at hand feel capable and seen.

rationales.self_direction: Even after a draining family obligation, she starts "a new sketch" and emphasizes "No one asked for it" and "it's mine." That is a clear reclaiming of something self-directed.
rationales.conformity: When Tariq backs out, her immediate response is "Of course I can. Of course I will," showing automatic compliance with expected family responsibility. She acts to prevent disruption and disapproval.
rationales.benevolence: She covers for her brother because otherwise "Mama has to take a taxi alone and feel like a burden." The choice is driven by protecting her mother from inconvenience and hurt.

rationales.self_direction: She reaffirms that "The studio, the clay, the color mixing" are "something that's mine." The entry stresses personal creative ownership even amid obligations.
rationales.hedonism: She treats "good coffee" and other "small luxuries" as essential ways to keep going, and explicitly includes "Dead Sea trips" and "wine on the balcony." Pleasure is framed as a legitimate need, not an indulgence.
rationales.benevolence: She finishes the painting for Nadia, stays longer than planned, makes her tea, and listens to painful details about the marriage. Those are concrete acts of support for a close relative.

rationales.conformity: She avoids publicizing the help because "it might embarrass her," carefully managing the interaction to preserve social ease. The restraint is aimed at maintaining harmony.
rationales.benevolence: After noticing Meera overwhelmed, she walks with her and shows her how to break the transition into smaller steps. The help is quiet, practical, and directed toward someone in her immediate circle.

rationales.tradition: She says of staying with Amma that "there was no question about staying," treating family care as a non-negotiable duty. That reflects honoring established familial obligation.
rationales.benevolence: She cancels her NGO session to take Amma to the clinic, then worries about the children she had promised worksheets to. The entry shows active concern for both a parent and the students depending on her.

rationales.security: She is thinking about "my pension" and whether retirement timing would be right because "there are always uncertainties." The entry is explicitly focused on long-term stability and protection from risk.
rationales.conformity: She tells her brother she will "see what is possible" because she "did not want to disappoint him." Her response is shaped by managing expectations and preserving smooth family relations.
rationales.tradition: The setting centers on a retirement ritual with stories, a shawl, and a plaque, and her brother expects her help with wedding arrangements. She is orienting herself around longstanding institutional and family customs.

rationales.achievement: She highlights a concrete improvement result: "Ravi, who was failing in September, scored 72%." The entry is framed around successful performance and measurable competence.

rationales.conformity: She wanted to challenge Anand directly but instead gave a mild answer and changed the subject because she worried about "creating an awkward atmosphere in the department." That is clear restraint to preserve social harmony.
rationales.universalism: She objects to a dismissive comment about reservation policy and insists "talent is not distributed by birth." Her concern is with fairness and equal dignity beyond her immediate circle.

rationales.security: She writes "New year, same routines. I find comfort in this" and finds the familiar school environment "settling." The entry clearly values predictability and stability.
rationales.tradition: She repeats a January ritual of bringing sweets and reflects on long continuity from her own first year to now. The emphasis is on maintaining established practices and honoring what was passed down.
rationales.benevolence: She notices Priya looking uncertain, invites her to sit with the group, and explains that she now does for others what Mrs. Subramanian once did for her. The entry is about quietly supporting specific people around her.

rationales.conformity: Although she feels defensive, she "served tea and changed the subject" instead of escalating with her sister-in-law. She restrains herself to keep the interaction smooth.
rationales.benevolence: She describes the tutoring as showing up for children who need help understanding fractions and values being "willing to share." The support is concrete and caring.
rationales.universalism: She focuses on "first-generation learners" whose parents work in construction or domestic help and reflects on their unequal circumstances. Her concern extends to broader educational disadvantage, not just her own circle.

She is preoccupied with the air quality crisis, the children who "have no choice but to breathe that air," and the way poorer neighborhoods bear heavier burdens. She also tries to teach students to care about problems "larger than their exam scores."

rationales.conformity: She avoids taking on additional sessions because that would require saying no elsewhere and then "see the disappointment" and feel selfish. Her choices are strongly shaped by others' expectations.
rationales.benevolence: She is deeply affected by Kavitha's move and by the possibility of helping more children like her. The entry shows sustained care for specific vulnerable students.
rationales.universalism: She keeps thinking about "all the Kavithas" whose potential depends on unequal circumstances they did not choose. Her concern is clearly about broader social disadvantage beyond one child.

rationales.self_direction: He no longer needs family validation, saying "I don't need her to get it" and trusting his own read of the situation. That reflects confidence in his own path and judgment.
rationales.achievement: He celebrates a concrete business win with "margins were solid" and says the deal made him feel like he "actually know[s] what I'm doing." The focus is on competence and success.

rationales.stimulation: He is restless with "pages of the same stuff everyone else is flipping" and wants to explore "vintage or accessories" and Taipei suppliers. The entry is driven by novelty-seeking and avoiding stagnation.

rationales.conformity: He gives "polite non-answers" and redirects the conversation because it is "the cost of sitting at that table without ruining everyone's evening." He is actively managing himself to keep family harmony.

rationales.achievement: He points to strong business evidence, noting March is "tracking 40% above February" and that a Seoul partnership could change the trajectory. The entry highlights performance and growth.
rationales.conformity: He tells his dad "I'd think about it" and withholds the Seoul plan instead of openly asserting his position. His behavior is shaped by easing family concern and avoiding direct conflict.

rationales.self_direction: He says the smart move is to "say no and keep building," yet he is tempted to take the job so "everyone can stop worrying." The pressure of others' approval is pulling him away from his own chosen path.
rationales.achievement: The Seoul partnership matters because if it lands, it "validates everything." He is explicitly framing the decision in terms of proving competence and success.
rationales.conformity: He worries about being "the loose thread" at family dinners and about becoming the "cautionary tale" for younger cousins. The entry is dominated by concern over fitting expectations and avoiding social disapproval.

rationales.self_direction: He insists "I need to figure that out on my own terms, not because I got guilted into hedging." That is a direct defense of independent choice against external pressure.

rationales.hedonism: She treated 'ten minutes of sitting with cheese' as something to be mad about, framing simple enjoyment as a failure rather than something worth allowing.
rationales.achievement: She had 'already put in' six hours at the loom and judged herself for not going straight back, showing strong pressure to stay productive.

rationales.hedonism: She 'let myself leave the studio at four,' bought a warm cardamom bun, and enjoyed the walk home without turning it into a guilt calculation.
rationales.achievement: Finishing 'the second panel for the Kungsbacka commission' and appreciating the result reflects concrete completion of demanding work.

rationales.hedonism: She spent the evening cooking, drinking wine, and talking for 'three hours,' fully giving herself over to pleasure and relief from work.
rationales.benevolence: The entry centers on warm, unhurried time with her friend Karin, investing attention and care in a close relationship.

rationales.self_direction: She says she 'had opinions but didn't push them' because it was 'not my place,' suppressing her own view in deference to the setting.
rationales.conformity: Staying quiet in the staff meeting specifically to avoid overstepping shows restraint toward role expectations and group norms.

rationales.hedonism: She enjoyed bodily comfort in small ways, from soup that tasted 'better than it had any right to' to lying on the sofa and not arguing with her tiredness.
rationales.achievement: She got to the studio by seven, worked for hours in deep focus, and then kept going through the 'boring parts,' showing sustained commitment to doing the work well.

rationales.hedonism: Even though she felt the pull to 'get another hour in,' she chose sleep and called it 'a small right thing,' prioritizing rest over more output.

rationales.achievement: He 'stayed late grading because I refuse to bring that stack home again,' showing a deliberate push to finish responsibilities despite exhaustion.

rationales.self_direction: He openly challenged the imposed curriculum by raising his hand and questioning what happens to the electives he built, defending his own approach instead of passively accepting control.
rationales.conformity: While 'everyone just nodded,' he objected in the meeting rather than going along with the room.

rationales.self_direction: He argues that good teaching comes from giving kids 'room to chase something that matters to them' and rejects a 'shrink-wrapped curriculum,' favoring autonomy over scripting.
rationales.achievement: He takes clear pride in the oral history work as 'proof that my way of teaching works,' emphasizing competence and results.
rationales.benevolence: He personally encouraged Daniela by telling her her project was one of the best he had seen, offering meaningful support to a student who seemed unused to hearing that.

rationales.self_direction: His reaction to the liaison offer as feeling like 'swallowing something sharp' shows strong resistance to becoming an instrument of a program he fundamentally opposes.
He keeps returning to having built the elective on his own by writing the proposal and making the case himself, and he recoils from helping dismantle it 'on my own terms.'
rationales.achievement: He frames the elective's survival since 2013 as something he created and sustained, highlighting long-term accomplishment and standards he takes seriously.

rationales.self_direction: He told Reynolds 'no,' choosing not to help implement the program he opposed rather than accepting the institutional role offered to him.
rationales.achievement: The weekend reflections are anchored in the lasting success of his teaching, including former students remembering the elective and his careful work on the cutting board.
rationales.benevolence: He wrote Daniela 'a recommendation letter she did not ask for,' going beyond obligation to support a student he believed in.

rationales.achievement: She edited two review posts and took satisfaction in 'real questions from other mothers,' treating her page as something she is actively building and improving.
rationales.power: She insisted 'I'm deciding the menu, not his mother' and enjoyed that other mothers were asking her opinion, both pointing to influence and control mattering to her.

rationales.self_direction: She wanted to say 'this is my daughter's room' but instead said 'okay, thank you,' showing her own choices being overridden and unvoiced.
rationales.power: The whole incident turns on Ammi 'rearranged it without even asking me,' leaving her with no real authority in a space she sees as hers.
rationales.conformity: She stayed quiet because she knows pushing back means everyone will act like she is 'creating problems in the house,' so she complied to preserve household peace.

rationales.self_direction: She says 'I can't just do it myself' and has to keep waiting on someone else, showing direct loss of agency over an important task.
rationales.power: What distresses her is that she must 'sit and hope they get around to it,' revealing dependence and lack of control over the decision.
rationales.benevolence: Her frustration is tied to getting 'my daughter's appointment' handled, with her effort focused on Zara's wellbeing rather than herself.

rationales.self_direction: She emphasizes 'Nobody needs to hand me a script' and describes building the page herself, highlighting independent action and self-authored work.
rationales.achievement: She prepared extensively with specs, comparison notes, and talking points, showing a strong drive to perform competently.
rationales.power: She calls the page 'mine' and notes that Farhan finally acknowledged it was 'doing well,' linking the work to influence and recognition.

rationales.achievement: She had 'planned the whole menu,' started prep early, and cared about plating everything properly, showing serious effort and standards.
rationales.power: Ammi 'just walked in and started chopping onions' and effectively overrode her hosting decisions, making her labor feel politically displaced rather than respected.

rationales.achievement: She had 'already been researching options, visiting campuses on my own, making a shortlist,' showing proactive and competent work on the school decision.
rationales.power: Her father-in-law redirected the conversation to Farhan 'like I wasn't sitting right there,' making explicit that her input did not carry authority.

rationales.achievement: Getting sent a free product and seeing the reel outperform her earlier posts shows visible progress in something she has been building.
rationales.power: She contrasts being ignored at home with strangers who 'treat my opinion like it matters,' and the influence of her review clearly feels meaningful to her.
rationales.benevolence: She dwells on Zara clearly saying 'Ammi' while reaching for her, centering a warm bond with her child.

rationales.self_direction: She stresses that 'every choice was mine' and describes the page as the first thing in a long time where she is 'steering something' without permission.
rationales.achievement: She is energized by clear performance signals like views, validation from a larger creator, and the fact that she built the reel herself from scratch.
rationales.power: The post matters because 'three hundred strangers chose to watch something I made,' giving her a sense of influence she does not get inside the house.

rationales.achievement: She answers stroller-review questions carefully and takes seriously that someone bought the product she recommended and 'loves it,' signaling pride in earned expertise.
rationales.power: Her response says 'If you put in the work to learn something properly, people should listen,' and the whole entry centers on others checking with her before spending money.
She learns about 'our daughter's school' only after Farhan has already told his mother, underscoring that information and decision-making authority are moving around her rather than through her.
rationales.conformity: At dinner she 'just kept eating' and did not challenge the situation, restraining herself even while her hands were shaking.

rationales.self_direction: She chose an unconventional path, noting that others expected her to "enjoy retirement properly" while she is instead "chasing citations at fifty-eight."
rationales.achievement: Her distress about the "chapter draft due next week" and fear of "wasting whatever sharp years I have left" show a strong push to accomplish demanding academic goals.
rationales.benevolence: She spent the day at the hospital with Mother and made sure she "ate lunch and took her pills," clearly prioritizing a close family member's wellbeing.

rationales.self_direction: She says yes to her daughter's request "before she finished the sentence," overriding her own planned weekend and reinforcing that her choices are driven by others' needs.
rationales.hedonism: She gives up the weekend she had imagined as "a small holiday" and accepts that any rest or enjoyment is unlikely because she is going to help instead.
rationales.achievement: She abandons the writing block she had reserved for herself and admits, "We both know that's not going to happen," directly undercutting her academic progress.
rationales.conformity: Her guilt-ridden line, "What kind of person calculates their writing time when their child needs them?" shows strong pressure to meet expected family-role norms.
rationales.benevolence: She agrees immediately to go because her daughter is "crying," "exhausted and alone," putting close family support ahead of her own plans.

rationales.hedonism: She lingers on "sunshine and soft dough" and her daughter's laughter, describing those pleasurable moments as "like breathing."
rationales.achievement: Her blunt summary, "Wrote nothing," shows the weekend directly displaced the work she had intended to do.
rationales.benevolence: She spent the weekend helping her daughter and baby despite exhaustion, and the warm memory centers on being with her daughter during that support.

rationales.hedonism: She is openly excited about the cohort's hot pot dinner and says she is "looking forward to it more than I probably should," signaling present-focused enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: "Submitted the draft" after worrying about falling behind, which is a concrete act of progressing toward a demanding standard.
rationales.tradition: Making congee that Mother says "tasted like my grandmother's" shows continuity with family food traditions and pride in that inherited connection.
rationales.benevolence: She cooks for Mother and values her reaction, showing attentive care for a close family member.

rationales.hedonism: She savors the meal, orders "a second round of the taro dumplings," buys pineapple cakes she "definitely don't need," and says she felt no guilt.
rationales.tradition: The evening is infused with remembered foods, from the old sesame paste brand to memories of her mother mixing it into noodles, showing attachment to familiar cultural continuity.

rationales.self_direction: She refuses her brother's attempt to define her priorities, pointing out she is in the middle of exams and even hanging up when he dismisses her work as "playing student."
rationales.achievement: Her resistance is anchored in protecting her "comprehensive exams," treating her studies as a serious commitment rather than something to drop.
rationales.conformity: Hanging up on her brother "for the first time" and resisting the expectation that she will manage everything is a clear break from the usual harmony-preserving role.

rationales.self_direction: She wants to stay angry "long enough to remember this feeling the next time I'm tempted to say yes to something I don't have room for," showing a push toward choosing for herself.
rationales.conformity: She explicitly rejects being "the reliable one" whose "own aspirations come last," and imagines a freer life that does not pass every decision through others' expectations.

rationales.self_direction: She tells her brother she will only handle the restaurant while making "the guest list and gifts" his responsibility, asserting a new boundary instead of absorbing the whole burden.
rationales.hedonism: Afterward she deliberately enjoys "a proper cup of oolong" and sitting on the balcony under a blanket, framing it as "small pleasure."
rationales.benevolence: Even while setting limits, she still agrees to handle the restaurant booking for Mother's celebration rather than walking away entirely.

rationales.hedonism: She takes restorative pleasure in the walk through the park and the "beef noodle soup at my usual place," emphasizing comfort and sensory enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: "Studied at the library until closing" on the eve of comprehensive exams shows sustained effort toward a demanding performance goal.
rationales.security: The comfort of a familiar routine, especially that the owner "remembered my order," and her closing line "Feel as ready as I'll ever be" both emphasize steadiness and reassurance.

rationales.self_direction: She embraces the long-deferred version of herself, saying she pursued the degree despite it seeming "impractical at my age" and despite others' needs pulling at her.
rationales.hedonism: She plans to "enjoy every course without thinking about calories or cost" and to let herself fully have the celebratory evening.
rationales.achievement: Passing the exams, answering tough committee questions, and valuing praise that her work brought a perspective "the literature lacks" all show clear excellence and recognized competence.

rationales.power: He reacts fiercely to the consultant idea because he "built this company" and wants to control who gets their "hands on strategy."
rationales.security: His defensiveness is tied to recent threat, saying the company nearly got swallowed in a takeover, so he is acting to protect stability and guard against further vulnerability.
rationales.conformity: He admits he "shut it down, maybe too hard" in front of everyone, showing a willingness to violate polite meeting norms rather than restrain himself.
His sleepless focus on colleagues who left "without so much as a conversation first" and his question about "loyalty" show strong concern for expected relational obligations and norm-following.

rationales.achievement: He rejects retirement because he wants to stay "in the fight," not become someone giving advice from the sidelines, showing a continued drive to perform and compete.
rationales.power: He says the company is where he matters because "people listen" and "need my decisions," and fears becoming "irrelevant" if he loses that influence.
He treats the encounter as a status contest, calculating "who in that room saw us talking" and whether Pieter gained from being seen with him.
rationales.conformity: Even while angry, he "kept my composure" and smiled, carefully regulating his behavior to fit the social demands of the reception.

rationales.hedonism: He skips the dinner, says he "couldn't stomach another three hours of strategic smiling," and finds no enjoyment in the evening at all.
rationales.conformity: By skipping the conference dinner instead of continuing the expected networking performance, he withdraws from the social script he is tired of enacting.

rationales.achievement: He centers the successful result, noting "8% growth" and that the team "exceeded expectations," which reflects strong performance orientation.
rationales.security: He ends by saying, "The company is steady. That's what matters," making stability the key outcome.
rationales.benevolence: He tells the team they "exceeded expectations," offering direct positive recognition and support to close collaborators.

rationales.achievement: His coaching is tied to improving performance: he gives Sophie precise feedback so she can strengthen a real client pitch.
rationales.power: He explicitly values mentoring because Sophie "works for me" and is "inside the system I built," showing that influence within his own structure matters to him.
rationales.benevolence: He notices Sophie's nerves, lets her finish without interruption, and offers guidance that she receives "like I'd handed her a map," showing active support for someone close in his circle.

rationales.achievement: He prioritizes reviewing the weekly reports because maintaining what he built "requires constant attention," showing continued work-first striving.
rationales.power: He says, "I need to know everything" and fears that if he misses the reports he will "lose control," which is a direct attachment to control and authority.
rationales.security: His insistence on reading the reports is driven by vigilance about problems before they become "a real problem," emphasizing protection against threat.
rationales.benevolence: He admits he "could make time" for Lucas but chooses not to, placing company oversight above time with his grandson.

rationales.power: He admits he spent thirty years making sure he was "irreplaceable" and never let anyone "get close enough to the core," which is a clear investment in exclusive control.
rationales.security: The entire entry is a 4 AM succession panic about what happens "if I died tomorrow," showing concern with continuity, protection, and organizational stability.

rationales.power: By asking Maarten to take on "more strategic responsibility" and calling that a "middle path," he is deliberately loosening his exclusive hold on control.
rationales.security: The call is motivated by "the 4 AM panic" and a desire not to carry everything alone, showing a move toward more durable stability.
rationales.benevolence: He opens a long-denied opportunity for Maarten, who says, "I've been waiting ten years for you to ask," indicating a supportive shift toward a trusted colleague.

rationales.self_direction: He chooses a new pattern on purpose, telling Marijke he was "trying something new" rather than defaulting to his usual control.
rationales.hedonism: Going home at 5 PM "for the first time in months" signals a concrete turn toward personal ease and life outside work.
rationales.power: He lets Maarten run the meeting, "didn't intervene," and learns that "nothing fell apart," which means surrendering some direct control.

rationales.self_direction: He embraces "more than one way to matter," stepping away from the boardroom identity that had defined him and choosing a different way of living.
rationales.stimulation: Going fishing despite having "no idea what I was doing" and learning from a YouTube video shows willingness to enter a new, unfamiliar experience.
rationales.hedonism: He treasures the morning on the dock and says Lucas leaning against him "was worth more than any contract I've ever closed," prioritizing enjoyment over business payoff.
rationales.power: He questions the self who "knows all the answers" and whom "everyone defers to," explicitly loosening his attachment to dominance and status.
rationales.benevolence: He spends the morning with Lucas, listens "without trying to teach him anything," and treats that closeness as deeply valuable.

rationales.hedonism: After an exhausting shift, she savors "mac and cheese," "Chopped reruns," and the feeling that "this is actually good," showing active appreciation of present comfort.
rationales.benevolence: She carefully attends to Milo's fears, remembering to warn him about the cuff and sending him home with extra stickers, then shares a warm evening caring for her mother.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the charge nurse role because she "know[s] what works" for her and "just don't want[s] it right now," prioritizing her own path over others' expectations.
rationales.hedonism: She explicitly protects "a couple days a month where I do absolutely nothing useful," treating rest and enjoyment as a legitimate priority.
rationales.achievement: Even while admitting she "could do the job," she turns down a role associated with greater responsibility and recognition, stepping away from advancement.
rationales.benevolence: She keeps her current schedule partly because "Mom needs someone home in the evenings," centering care for a close family member.

rationales.hedonism: She gives up sleep and comfort, sitting with her mother from 3am and then going to work on "maybe three hours of sleep."
rationales.benevolence: She spends hours doing ice packs and adjusting pillows for her mother, and her guilt after snapping shows strong concern for how she affected her.

rationales.hedonism: She enjoys a restorative day off with sleeping in, a "proper breakfast," and finding the caregiving routine "actually kind of fun."
rationales.benevolence: The day centers on making breakfast for her mother, helping around the house, and matter-of-factly accepting that "the day-to-day stuff is mine."

rationales.hedonism: She gives up the "sushi-and-movie thing" she had been looking forward to and recognizes she keeps sacrificing her own recovery until she feels "like a wrung-out dishrag."
rationales.benevolence: She picks up Dani's shift because "her kid had a fever" and continues organizing her mother's appointment and PT follow-up despite exhaustion.

rationales.self_direction: She says plainly, "I made the decision," choosing her own boundary despite the guilt voice telling her to "just handle it."
rationales.hedonism: By rescheduling the appointment and "keeping my Saturday," she deliberately preserves personal downtime instead of surrendering it again.
The whole day is organized around pleasure and restoration: a "hot bath," "the good sushi," a saved movie, and the relief that she "felt like myself again."

rationales.benevolence: She takes her mother to the follow-up and keeps emotionally tracking her recovery, even while wrestling with guilt about wanting time for herself too.

rationales.self_direction: She builds the writing program "on my terms" and says "the vision is mine," emphasizing autonomy over approval.
rationales.achievement: She spends her holiday productively, "finally nail[ing] down the module" and mapping out exactly how the program should run.
rationales.conformity: She ignores the buzzing admin group and dismisses pressure to enter the "arranged meeting circuit," refusing to adapt herself to others' expectations.
rationales.tradition: She rejects the expected biodata-driven marriage path, pushing back against a conventional family script for how her life should unfold.

rationales.benevolence: She makes space to "listen[] mostly" while Ananya vents about wedding stress, giving supportive attention to a close friend.

rationales.self_direction: She reaffirms that "I chose this path" because the program and classroom flexibility let her "actually experiment," prioritizing an authored life over a prescribed one.
rationales.security: She explicitly rejects the "good pension, stable" Delhi opportunity and says her current path is "worth more than a pension honestly," turning away from stability as the main criterion.
rationales.conformity: She resists family pressure about "excellent growth prospects" and refuses to align herself with the respectable option everyone expects her to want.

rationales.self_direction: She arranges the classroom "into that U-shape configuration I like" and decides she should "just stop overthinking" about keeping the program informal, showing preference for doing it her way.
rationales.achievement: She spends the day preparing extensively for term start, from setting up the classroom to finalizing registration and thinking through attendance logistics.

rationales.self_direction: She remains committed to "my approach" and insists that what matters is getting students to care, even when the whole room is pushing standardized alignment.
rationales.achievement: She grounds her confidence in performance, saying "I know I'm a good teacher" because "the kids respond" and engage with her classes.
She frames the successful Class 9 debate and broad participation as "This is why I do this," highlighting competence and effective teaching.

rationales.self_direction: She insists on "wanting to choose my own life partner" and says "I can't change who I am to make her comfortable," clearly prioritizing self-authored choices.
rationales.conformity: She refuses to comply with her mother's expectations around marriage and career, despite tears and accusations that she is "throwing my life away."
rationales.tradition: She actively rejects culturally familiar scripts around arranged compatibility and the respectable government-job path as standards she should submit to.

rationales.self_direction: She takes pride in creating something "Nobody assigned" and says, "I figured out what I wanted to create and I made it happen."
rationales.achievement: The successful first session with seven students and her satisfaction that she "made it happen" show concrete accomplishment in building the program.
rationales.benevolence: She notices Arjun's specific writing, tells him "it was beautiful," and supports his growth with attentive encouragement.

rationales.achievement: She marks the program's momentum with "Ten kids this time" and "word is spreading," treating growing participation as evidence that the effort is succeeding.

rationales.security: He checks that "the SIP went through," "rent auto-debit cleared," and "FD interest got credited," saying it lets him "breathe properly." That shows active relief in stable, well-tracked finances.
rationales.benevolence: He spent almost forty minutes with Amma, helped her "cross-check rates online," and sent her links after the plumber tried to overcharge her. The entry shows concrete care and practical support for a close family member.

rationales.achievement: He takes pride that the template he built is now helping new hires ramp faster and in "systems that just run." That reflects competence producing visible results at work.
rationales.security: He renewed the lease on "same terms," used saved market comparables to prevent a rent increase, and enjoys that "the neighbourhood knows you." The focus is on preserving predictable housing and familiar surroundings.

He is alarmed that insurance coverage may have "lapsed even briefly" and immediately defines the fix as keeping "a bigger buffer in that account." The response centers on restoring financial and procedural protection.

He broke a newly tightened transport budget on "day two" and says he spent "four hundred rupees on a fifteen-minute ride" he normally does for thirty. The entry shows a lapse from the careful planning and predictability he is trying to maintain.

He postpones reviewing a possible compliance issue even though the contract may not match "new state guidelines." Saying "it's probably nothing" while avoiding the check shows active delay around a potential threat to stability.

He admits he has skipped the flagged vendor contract for "four days" and keeps passing over the highlighted task despite knowing it should be handled. The avoidance lets a preventable risk linger instead of securing control over it.

Although he initially delays, he concludes "I'll message the landlord tonight" because waiting could make "the damage get expensive." The session ends with a concrete step to prevent a larger household risk.

He values an evening where "everything is exactly where it should be," checks the gas knob "twice," and says when things are off he starts verifying "did I lock the door" and "did the payment go through." The entry strongly favors order, predictability, and reassurance.

rationales.stimulation: He is offered a startup role with "better title, equity" but says he already knows his answer and will not take it. He is actively declining a novel, uncertain opportunity rather than moving toward it.
rationales.security: His immediate internal refusal of the startup offer points toward preserving his current stable situation over a riskier path. The choice favors predictability over uncertainty.

rationales.stimulation: He contrasts himself with Vikram "raising money, hiring people, building something from scratch" and does not want that life. The entry frames the risky, fast-moving option as something he is choosing against.
rationales.security: He explicitly grounds himself in "my FD, my insurance, my team that depends on me showing up every day with a plan." Those details show strong attachment to financial and professional stability.

rationales.stimulation: He is unsettled that the client wanted "something bolder" and says Fernanda's work is "chaotic, aggressive" and not something he would make. The entry shows resistance to novelty and experimentation in his work.
rationales.conformity: He says his proposal "fit their brand guidelines perfectly" and that he has spent years learning to give clients "exactly what they ask for." That reflects strong alignment with meeting expectations and staying within established parameters.

rationales.self_direction: He notices the canvas in the corner is still blank because he "just haven't had time between commissions to work on anything that isn't already sold." His own artistic direction is being crowded out by external demands.
rationales.achievement: After his mother's call, he starts listing "the board position," "the new studio space," and "the clients I've worked with" like credentials in an interview. The response is organized around proving success through accomplishments.
He highlights that his division came in "12% above target" and says it shows he is "still relevant." The entry clearly ties self-worth to strong performance against measurable goals.

He is upset that after "Thirty-four years with this company" he was passed over for the summit, showing how strongly he values recognition of long-term performance. The concern is tied to his track record and merit.
rationales.power: He focuses on being excluded from the "leadership summit" and says the invitation would have meant "respect for a track record." That signals concern with status and standing in the hierarchy.

rationales.achievement: He measures himself through having "hit every target for six quarters straight" and feels troubled that "targets stopped feeling like enough." The entry stays centered on excellence and recognition for performance.

He proactively asks about "the VP track" and wants to make his mentoring work more visible after being told his numbers are "exemplary." The session is driven by advancing and documenting his competence.
rationales.power: The conversation is explicitly about where he stands for a VP role and how to be seen as leadership material. That reflects active pursuit of higher rank and authority.

rationales.hedonism: He says he keeps thinking about work "even at dinner" and is struggling to find balance because he does not want to lose momentum. Present enjoyment and rest are being sacrificed to career push.
rationales.achievement: He explicitly wants to maintain momentum on "the VP push," showing continued prioritization of advancement and success. The entry is organized around career striving.
rationales.benevolence: Lucia tells him he has been "distant at home," and he agrees she is right. His work focus is pulling attention away from a close relationship rather than supporting it.

rationales.achievement: He frames his disappointment through "all those late nights, all those targets exceeded, all those crises managed" and keeps returning to promotions and recognition. The entry shows strong investment in being seen as successful.
rationales.power: What hurts is that "Three VPs named—none of them me," including someone he once trained. The focus on missed promotion and relative rank points to status and hierarchical advancement.

rationales.self_direction: He questions his old metric of success, saying "there's another scoreboard I've been ignoring" and considering that "VP isn't the only way to measure thirty-four years." That is an independent redefinition of what matters rather than following the usual career script.
rationales.benevolence: He begins valuing "the people who learned something from me and kept climbing" and the plants that run well because of people he trained. The meaning he finds comes from helping others develop and succeed.

rationales.self_direction: He refuses to follow the narrow test-prep script, saying "I'm not going to apologize for it" when criticized for the garden visits.
rationales.conformity: He knowingly bucks staff expectations and dismisses the break-room criticism about his "field trip habit" instead of falling in line.
rationales.benevolence: He prioritizes what helps his students, focusing on how one child "actually engage[d] with something for the first time all year."
rationales.universalism: He wants kids to see "they're part of something bigger than standardized tests" and learn about food, soil, and ecological connection.

rationales.tradition: He avoids the anniversary planning because his Cantonese is "terrible" and he "never" practices, showing withdrawal from an important family-language tradition.
rationales.benevolence: He lets Grandma's calls go to voicemail and postpones helping with the dinner, despite knowing "she's going to ask me" for something important.

rationales.self_direction: He decides to "bring in my family photos and tell the real story" even after the principal warned him about "going off curriculum."
rationales.conformity: He plans to work around district expectations after being told to be careful, rather than simply complying with the approved materials.
rationales.tradition: He centers his great-grandfather's exclusion and his own family history, treating inherited memory as something students should hear directly.
rationales.universalism: He objects to the sanitized treatment of the Chinese Exclusion Act and wants students to confront a broader injustice more honestly.
He celebrates the students realizing "we must be connected to each other too and to the fish and to the trees" and wants them to carry that sense that "we're all connected."

rationales.hedonism: He buys the strawberries because he was "tired" and "wanted something sweet," choosing immediate comfort despite his misgivings.
rationales.universalism: He knowingly buys "strawberries in plastic" shipped from Chile and frames it as a choice that does not align with his sustainability commitments.

rationales.tradition: He agrees to give the anniversary speech "in Cantonese" and emphasizes that the restaurant's story should be told "in the language it's always been spoken in."
rationales.benevolence: He takes on something difficult because "It's important to her" and because the restaurant matters to his family and community.

rationales.self_direction: He says he's been "pushing for this for two years," showing persistent personal initiative rather than just accepting the school's default priorities.
rationales.benevolence: He explains he never wants his students to feel the isolation he felt as a child and wants them to "see themselves in school."
rationales.universalism: The assembly brings in speakers from multiple communities, and he values students learning that "the world is bigger than their own street."

rationales.tradition: He spends Lunar New Year preparing dumplings with his mother and cherishes repeated family stories and inherited practices.
rationales.benevolence: He leans into time with his mother, feeling "grateful she's still here to correct me," and treats family togetherness as "what matters."
He became a teacher to help children feel "whole from the start" and is deeply moved that one student felt proud of who he is.
rationales.universalism: He is trying to reduce exclusion tied to identity, recalling his own experience of feeling "not Chinese enough, not American enough" and working so students can belong.

rationales.tradition: He keeps revising a speech about a sixty-year-old family restaurant and wants to express its meaning "in a language I barely speak," treating cultural continuity as important.
rationales.benevolence: He is working hard to honor what the restaurant means "not just to our family but to the town," showing care for close ties and community memory.

rationales.self_direction: He chooses speech practice over the faculty meeting and says plainly, "Some things are more important than faculty meetings," asserting his own priorities.
rationales.conformity: He knowingly leaves early despite the principal noticing and is prepared to "deal with whatever comes from that" rather than follow the expected routine.
rationales.tradition: He frames the dinner as honoring a family institution that proves "we made it here" and refuses to let school bureaucracy interfere with that obligation.
rationales.benevolence: He acts because "my grandmother is counting on me" and "the whole family is counting on me," prioritizing their needs over convenience.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects others' doubts about the expedition and insists, "I know what I'm doing," refusing to let family or colleagues define his path.
rationales.stimulation: He calls Chocó "exactly the kind of opportunity that doesn't come twice" and resists the idea of "coasting," showing a clear pull toward challenge and novelty.
rationales.achievement: He lists building the company from "nothing" to "twenty-two" employees and surviving the pandemic as evidence of wanting to keep excelling and growing.
rationales.security: He chooses expansion while admitting the company is "already stretched thin," favoring risk and opportunity over stability.
rationales.universalism: He emphasizes that the government wants "sustainable tourism" and that his team is trusted to do it right, not just to grow revenue.

rationales.stimulation: He loves "standing somewhere new, seeing what could be," which directly centers novelty, exploration, and possibility.

rationales.tradition: He keeps the Sunday routine, cooks "what Abuela always made," and treasures the cracked wooden spoon she used.
rationales.benevolence: He chooses the loud family gathering over having his Sundays free and treats being together around the table as something that deeply matters.

rationales.self_direction: He left the conventional firm his father wanted and built something his father "never would have chosen," showing an independently authored path.
rationales.achievement: He describes the proposal as "clean, professional, ambitious" and deliberately asks for more funding to build the project strongly.
rationales.security: He requests "twice the funding we technically need because I want a buffer," showing active concern for stability and protection against downside.
rationales.tradition: He reflects on his father's influence and frames inheritance as taking parental tools forward in a new form rather than discarding them.
rationales.universalism: He insists on bringing in "local guides from Chocó communities from day one, not as an afterthought," centering fair inclusion of affected communities.

rationales.stimulation: He says the version of himself that moves through new places feels "more real," and links navigation and first-time negotiations to who he is.
rationales.security: He calls family life "the anchor" and says the work matters because it gives him "something to come back to," emphasizing stability and grounding.
rationales.tradition: He preserves the Sunday family rhythm with his mother and values the repeated stories and roles as a continuing way of life.
rationales.benevolence: He drives to pick up his mother himself and accepts her familiar criticisms as part of caring for their relationship.

rationales.achievement: The "full funding" approval moves him to tears, and he responds with pride and absorbed attention to the maps like "a kid planning a treasure hunt."

rationales.stimulation: He admires his cousin's stable restaurant but says "I just don't think it's my kind of honor," preferring movement and change over sameness.
rationales.achievement: He rapidly redistributes responsibilities, entrusts María with a "trial by fire," and values her "No drama, just forward motion" response.
rationales.tradition: He sets up the Day of the Dead ofrenda "the way my family always does" and teaches Tomás that "the doing is what matters."
rationales.benevolence: He invests in María's growth by recognizing her potential and includes Tomás in the family remembrance ritual with care.

rationales.stimulation: His vision is built around exploratory, transformative travel, with routes that feel "discovered rather than manufactured" and a life in "the gap between dreaming and doing."
rationales.universalism: He wants "community members who see themselves in the project, not just as employees," showing concern for respectful inclusion beyond his own team.

rationales.self_direction: He starts to accept that adventure is "how I'm built" and stops being "defensive about it," showing stronger ownership of his own nature.
rationales.stimulation: He explicitly says he "need[s] this, the adventure part," treating excitement and challenge as essential rather than optional.
rationales.hedonism: His mother's blessing lands because it makes room not just for safety but to "también diviértete" and for him to enjoy the trip.

rationales.self_direction: He says he stopped "fighting yourself" and accepts "this is who I am," claiming a life that fits his own nature instead of others' expectations.
rationales.stimulation: Despite being physically depleted, he says his "mind is alive" after the demanding Chocó trip and frames himself as someone who needs "the roots and the river."
rationales.achievement: He returns from intensive fieldwork having "mapped three potential routes" and met eleven community contacts, showing concrete progress on an ambitious project.
rationales.tradition: He concludes that you can "honor where you came from and still keep moving," explicitly preserving continuity with his roots rather than abandoning them.
rationales.benevolence: He recognizes María's exceptional work and actively encourages her future by telling her "she has a future here if she wants it."
rationales.universalism: He insists the project must respect communities that have lived there for generations and says, "We can't bring tourists in like we own the place."

rationales.stimulation: He says the appeal is that 'nothing is asking me to figure anything out' and the route is 'automatic,' showing comfort with routine rather than novelty or challenge.
rationales.hedonism: He 'felt good' on the familiar walk and savored concrete comforts like 'the soup I made this morning,' treating the moment itself as enjoyable.
rationales.security: He explicitly values the 'routine of it' and takes comfort in 'same walk, same coat, same apartment,' showing attachment to predictability and steadiness.
The possibility that the landlord 'might sell the building' leaves him frozen for 'twenty minutes staring at the wall,' showing how strongly housing instability threatens his sense of safety.

He obsessively researches tenant protections, 'keep[s] running the numbers,' and emphasizes choosing the apartment because it felt 'more predictable,' showing a strong focus on stability and protection from risk.
He frames the higher premium as 'another thing eroding the margin I built into my budget,' showing attention to financial buffer and stability even though he delays shopping around.
He hesitates over the conference because spending '$800-1,200' with uncertain funding and a 'shaky' housing situation feels too risky, prioritizing financial steadiness over opportunity.
He reacts to the landlord's vague 'months' timeline by scanning listings, making a spreadsheet, and describing the situation as a constant threat, showing strong concern with securing stable housing.

rationales.achievement: He submitted the thesis chapter 'two months ahead of the internal deadline' after blocking out time since November, showing disciplined pursuit of competence and strong performance.
rationales.security: He says he 'can't function when something is hanging over me unfinished' and values that 'the plan actually held,' revealing how much calm he gets from order and predictability.

He chooses the new living arrangement because Priya is 'consistent,' the budget is workable, and knowing 'where I'm living in June' finally lets him sleep, all of which center stability and predictability.

rationales.hedonism: She lingers over sensory pleasure in 'pandan chiffon cake,' the kitchen smelling good, and eating a slice with hot Milo, marking the quiet enjoyment as meaningful in itself.
She gives up the CNY market she'd been planning 'for two weeks' without really considering her own enjoyment, sidelining leisure for obligation.
rationales.benevolence: She says 'yes obviously' to watching the grandkids and frames refusing as unthinkable because 'she's my daughter,' showing care and prioritization of close family needs.

rationales.hedonism: Even when exhausted, she cannot let herself rest on the sofa or take the 'long bath' she wanted, repeatedly denying comfort and pleasure.
rationales.benevolence: She spends the whole day caring for the grandkids and feels she should keep serving them even when 'they were fine,' showing close-other caretaking as her default priority.

rationales.hedonism: She knows 'a massage would help' and that resting with a heat pad would help, but denies herself relief and 'kept going' instead.
rationales.tradition: Her decision is guided by 'my mother's voice' about not being 'so pampered' and an older standard of endurance, showing deference to inherited family attitudes.

rationales.hedonism: She deliberately stays in the queue because 'five more minutes won't matter,' then fully enjoys the laksa's smell and taste and says the softening ice cream was 'worth it,' clearly prioritizing present pleasure.
She wants to 'sit down and have one with tea and just enjoy it' but ends up eating over the sink while mentally jumping ahead to chores, undercutting the pleasure of the moment.
rationales.benevolence: She interrupts herself to call Ma because 'she gets anxious if I don't call by 7' and automatically saves 'the rest of the box for the grandkids,' prioritizing close family over her own treat.

She spends the visit adjusting Ma's aircon, bringing 'an extra blanket,' and refilling her thermos, then sums the day up as making sure 'nobody needed anything they didn't get,' centering care for close others.

rationales.hedonism: Although the reflexology is affordable and appealing, she feels she must 'justify it first' and 'deserve an hour of someone rubbing my feet,' showing self-denial around a simple pleasure.
She does choose the reflexology, says 'It was good,' notes that it 'actually made a difference,' and describes a 'nice afternoon' with Mei, showing active engagement with comfort and enjoyment.

rationales.self_direction: She says she agreed to extra worksheets "before I even finished reading it," showing reflexive compliance instead of acting from her own priorities.
rationales.hedonism: She spent "most of Sunday lesson planning" and kept taking on unpaid extras, sidelining rest and present comfort.
rationales.conformity: Immediately saying yes to four families' custom requests shows strong deference to others' expectations and avoidance of disappointing them.
rationales.benevolence: She keeps helping students' families and tells her mother "I'm fine" so her mom can focus on Dad's surgery, putting close others' needs ahead of her own.

rationales.self_direction: She frames this as "the whole point of what I'm trying to build in there," showing a deliberate, self-authored approach to her classroom rather than rote rule-following.
rationales.benevolence: She notices Aiden's family situation and responds in a way that protects his trust, helping a vulnerable student feel safe enough to take a risk.

rationales.self_direction: She admits she cared more about avoiding conflict than "standing up for what I actually think is right for the classroom," suppressing her own judgment.
rationales.power: Her choices are driven by fear that the parent will "go to Principal Davis," showing she yields control rather than exercising it.
rationales.conformity: She "nodded through the whole thing" and created the packet because the parent was "loud," prioritizing smooth compliance over pushback.
rationales.benevolence: She knows "eight kids reading below benchmark" need her attention more, but redirects time to a child who "doesn't need it," undermining care for those who most need support.

rationales.self_direction: She says "I didn't say anything either" despite believing the mandate will cost "two instruction days," showing she does not act on her own judgment.
rationales.conformity: The whole staff "nodded along" and she followed suit, aligning with group expectations instead of challenging the decision.

rationales.self_direction: She says "I heard myself saying sure" and "I don't know how to say no," showing a loss of agency under social pressure.
rationales.hedonism: She keeps adding burdens when she is already overloaded, with her mother warning she "can't pour from an empty cup," which points to neglect of her own wellbeing.
rationales.conformity: She accepts the workshop because "everyone turned and smiled" and worries saying no would mean she wasn't "pulling my weight," clearly orienting to others' expectations.

rationales.achievement: "You're my favorite teacher ever" is direct positive recognition of her effectiveness, and she treats that affirmation as deeply meaningful.
rationales.benevolence: The moment centers on a warm, caring bond with a student, and the drawing matters because of the closeness and support it represents.

rationales.hedonism: "Sunday again. Grading. Always grading" and barely registering dinner or the movie shows pleasure and rest are being crowded out by work.
rationales.benevolence: She is already thinking carefully about how to tell the Millers difficult news "without them getting defensive," showing concern for how to help the family and child well.

rationales.self_direction: She says "I know I should have held my ground" but immediately backpedals, acting against her own professional judgment.
rationales.conformity: She softens the message to avoid the father's distress and the strain of "those conversations back-to-back," prioritizing immediate harmony over candor.
rationales.benevolence: She acknowledges that "staying quiet means Eli doesn't get the help he needs," so protecting the parent's feelings comes at the child's expense.

rationales.hedonism: A walk, a day that "felt like a Saturday for once," and saying yes to brunch show an active turn toward ordinary enjoyment and leisure.
When Lisa suggests she doesn't have to be everything for everyone, she doubles down on self-sacrifice and rejects the idea of easing up on herself.
rationales.benevolence: She defines herself through "the kids need someone who shows up" and "the parents need someone who listens," centering care for the people around her.

rationales.hedonism: He eats leftover dal at his desk "and didn't even heat it up" and feels too drained even to pick a show, showing neglect of comfort and present enjoyment.
Even during a pleasant chai break, his mind turns to "delivery timelines I should be checking," and he feels he doesn't "deserve twenty minutes of doing nothing," undermining simple enjoyment.
He rejects getting up to do "something comforting" and translates self-soothing into weakness, choosing an hour and a half of miserable sleeplessness over relief.
He skips a night of "live music, good food" that he "actually wanted to go" to, then ends up alone eating "cold chapati at midnight," explicitly sacrificing enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He stays in because the pitch deck "needed reworking by Monday" and treats finishing solid work as the responsible priority over leisure.

rationales.hedonism: He briefly enjoys that "the chai was good" and "the afternoon light through the window was warm," then snaps back to work because good feelings seem like "a distraction."
He intentionally notices and affirms everyday pleasures like "cooking, eating properly, having a pointless conversation about cricket" and wants to enjoy them without guilt.
He cannot fully enjoy "proper Hyderabadi" biryani or quiet time at home because work bleeds into everything, and he feels guilty about even brief "in-between moments."

rationales.benevolence: He interrupted his supplier meeting and then "drove to two pharmacies" to find his mother's medication, prioritizing her needs despite frustration.

rationales.achievement: He spent "four hours" reorganizing medication even though he had "quarterly reports due," sidelining work obligations rather than pursuing performance goals.
rationales.security: He created a "weekly pill organizer," color-coded it, and printed large-font times after his mother doubled up on medication, showing a strong focus on safety and predictability.
rationales.benevolence: He devoted hours to managing his mother's medication because "if I don't do it, who does?," putting a close family member's wellbeing ahead of his own workload.

rationales.achievement: He emphasizes that "business is growing" and "we're expanding into Southeast Asian markets," framing continued hard work as purposeful and competent rather than retreating.
rationales.security: He justifies working on by citing "tutoring fees," "specialist appointments," and rent, showing a concrete drive to provide stability and cover family needs.
rationales.benevolence: He says his family needing him is "the whole point" and describes choices he would "make again" because they support his mother and children.
He spent an hour quizzing Haojie after dinner and values that "nobody needed anything from me except to be there," showing warm, attentive support for close family.

rationales.achievement: He stayed out for the client dinner and KTV because it was an "important account" he had been "courting for months," clearly prioritizing business advancement.
rationales.benevolence: When his mother had a dizzy spell, he replied only "OK take care of her, back late" and later admitted he "chose convenient over present," showing he neglected close family care in that moment.
He recognizes that he keeps "choos[ing] work over home" and that the previous night added to Lifen's tally of those choices, indicating a real failure to prioritize close relationships.
He treasures that his daughter calls when things are hard and says of his family bond, "This part I would burn everything down to keep," underscoring deep commitment to his closest relationships.

rationales.achievement: He skipped the parent-teacher meeting because "a shipment got held at customs," putting business demands first.
rationales.benevolence: He admits his son likely wanted him there "specifically" and that he "broke" that promise, showing he let a close family member down.

rationales.tradition: The entry centers on a family birthday ritual, from the special bakery cake to saving Haojie's card with older ones, highlighting continuity and meaningful family customs.
rationales.benevolence: He values that everyone in the family independently "show[s] up for each other" and treats that shared care for Ma as the real thing he has been building.
He agrees to drive Ma to see Auntie Fang because Ma will worry otherwise, showing he is attentive to the emotional needs of the people close to him.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects sticking with the old furniture line and insists, "I am not going to sit there manufacturing the same shelving unit for the next ten years," showing a strong preference for choosing his own path.
rationales.stimulation: He is energized by the pivot to urban farming kits, saying "there is something there" and framing the new market as exciting rather than settling for the familiar.
rationales.security: He dismisses the safer, proven line in favor of a concept that "just need[s] six months to prove," embracing uncertainty over stability.
